# Architecture

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It records
the design choices for the crate and the 0.1.0 starting point.
Code structure sections will grow as the ladder lands; the
choices below are the founding state (2026-07-21, pre-scaffold).

## Overview

The crate is a `no_std`, no-alloc reimplementation of the
HdrHistogram log-linear bucketing scheme (Gil Tene et al.,
[HdrHistogram](https://github.com/HdrHistogram/HdrHistogram)),
sized and shaped for tprobe-class continuous monitoring: tens
to hundreds of probes per process, each recording tick-counter
deltas run-to-completion, analyzed at the end of the run.

- The one hot operation is `record(value)`: find the bucket
  (clz + shift), saturating-increment its counter. No floats,
  no allocation, no panics, no data-dependent work.
- Everything else — quantiles, merge, iteration — is
  O(buckets), `no_std`-capable, and off the measured path.
- Lifecycle for 0.1.0 is run-to-completion: create, record,
  analyze at end. No snapshot/swap/reset API yet (see
  [Deferred directions](#deferred-directions)).

## Parameterization (h2)

The h2 restatement of HdrHistogram's bucketing — borrowed from
iopsystems' [histogram](https://github.com/iopsystems/histogram)
crate, written up at [h2histogram.org](https://h2histogram.org/)
— replaces `(lowest, highest, significant_figures)` with
two powers, chosen per instance at runtime:

- `grouping_power` (g) — each power-of-two range is split
  into 2ᵍ linear sub-buckets; relative value error ≤ 2⁻ᵍ.
- `max_value_power` (n) — max trackable value is 2ⁿ−1;
  over-range values clamp into the top bucket (for lateness
  detection the overflow *is* the signal).
- Values below 2ᵍ⁺¹ are recorded exactly.
- `buckets = (n − g + 1) · 2ᵍ`.

## Storage: config / counts split

The load-bearing structural choice: config and counts storage
are separate, and the core histogram type *borrows* its counts.

- `Config` — the two powers plus derived index math; `const fn`
  so callers can size storage at compile time.
- `Histogram<'a, C>` — config + `&'a mut [C]` counts; no
  derived state (totals are summed at read time, keeping the
  record path minimal). The caller owns the memory: a static
  buffer, a stack array, or (with `std`) a heap slice.
- `HistogramArray<const N, C>` — owned inline `[C; N]`
  convenience wrapper with a size check against
  `Config::total_buckets()`. Rust's stable const generics
  can't derive N from (g, n) directly (`generic_const_exprs`
  is unstable), so N is explicit and checked.
- Why borrowing is the default: the future buffer-swap model —
  a background task handing a probe a fresh zeroed slice and
  taking the full one — needs detachable storage. Keeping
  config and counts unfused now means that model lands later
  without reshaping the type.

## Counts

- Counter type is generic (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`) with
  **u32 the default**; increments saturate — no panic, no
  wraparound lies.
- We think u32 is the right monitoring default because a
  deployment that watches stability will bound per-histogram
  totals (by run length now, by snapshot interval later);
  u64 is for run-forever accumulation and doubles the
  footprint for it.

## Feature map

- Core: `#![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)]`,
  no dependencies.
- `std` (default): convenience only — the demo example and
  any std-needing helpers. Band-table reporting stays in
  tprobe; this crate does not duplicate it.
- Dev-dependencies (tests/benches only): iopsystems
  `histogram` and `hdrhistogram` 7 as correctness oracles,
  plus a bench harness.

## API sketch (provisional)

```rust
pub struct Config { /* grouping_power, max_value_power */ }
impl Config {
    pub const fn new(g: u8, n: u8) -> Result<Config, Error>;
    pub const fn total_buckets(&self) -> usize;
    pub const fn index_for(&self, value: u64) -> usize;
    pub const fn value_range(&self, index: usize) -> (u64, u64);
}

pub struct Histogram<'a, C: Counter = u32> {
    // config, counts: &'a mut [C]
}
impl<C: Counter> Histogram<'_, C> {
    pub fn record(&mut self, value: u64);
    pub fn record_n(&mut self, value: u64, count: u64);
    pub fn quantile(&self, q: f64) -> Option<u64>;
    pub fn merge_from(&mut self, other: &Self) -> Result<(), Error>;
    pub fn buckets(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Bucket>;
}

pub struct HistogramArray<const N: usize, C: Counter = u32> {
    // owned [C; N], checked against Config::total_buckets()
}
```

## Size tradeoffs

`bytes = (n − g + 1) · 2ᵍ · sizeof(C)` + ~48 B header
(min/max/config if the hot-path extras are adopted).

What a max value of 2ⁿ ticks spans in time depends on the tick
source's rate:

| n  | 100 Hz  | 1 kHz   | 3 GHz   |
|----|---------|---------|---------|
| 16 | ~11 min | ~66 s   | ~22 µs  |
| 20 | ~2.9 h  | ~17 min | ~350 µs |
| 24 | ~1.9 d  | ~4.7 h  | ~5.6 ms |
| 32 | ~1.4 y  | ~50 d   | ~1.4 s  |
| 36 | ~22 y   | ~2.2 y  | ~23 s   |
| 40 | ~349 y  | ~35 y   | ~6 min  |

A slow tick source therefore wants a much smaller n, which
shrinks the footprint directly: e.g. a 1 kHz tick watching
1 ms..65 s lateness needs only n = 16 — at g = 4/u32 that is
(16−4+1)·2⁴ = 208 buckets ≈ 832 B per probe.

| g  | n  | C   | rel. err | buckets | bytes   |
|----|----|-----|----------|---------|---------|
| 2  | 32 | u16 | 25%      | 124     | ~250 B  |
| 4  | 36 | u32 | 6.3%     | 528     | ~2.1 KB |
| 6  | 40 | u32 | 1.6%     | 2,240   | ~9 KB   |
| 7  | 40 | u32 | 0.8%     | 4,352   | ~17 KB  |
| 7  | 40 | u64 | 0.8%     | 4,352   | ~34 KB  |
| 10 | 40 | u32 | 0.1%     | 31,744  | ~124 KB |

- rel. err is the value-quantization bound 2⁻ᵍ: each
  power-of-two range holds 2ᵍ equal-width buckets, so a
  bucket spans ≤ 2⁻ᵍ of the values in it. Counts are exact;
  only the value axis is quantized — any value read back
  (quantile, boundary) is within 2⁻ᵍ of the recorded one.
  Values below 2ᵍ⁺¹ are exact (width-1 buckets).
  - 2⁻ᵍ is the full uncertainty band: reporting a bucket
    endpoint gives one-sided error up to 2⁻ᵍ; reporting the
    midpoint halves it to ±2⁻ᵍ⁄2. Which convention
    `quantile()` uses is decided at 0.1.0-5 (oracle parity
    with `hdrhistogram`'s upper-bound convention weighs in).
- g=7/u32 is parity with `hdrhistogram` at 2 significant
  figures; g=10 approximates 3.
- We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot: error is on
  the *value* axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× does
  not need 2-sigfig fidelity.
- Fleet math: 100 probes at g=6/n=40/u32 ≈ 900 KB per
  process; double it if a snapshot model later
  double-buffers each probe.

## Validation

## Readout requirements (band tables)

The consumer-side bar is iiac-perf's band table: z/p/n quantile
bands, each showing first/last/range/count/mean, plus overall
and quantile-trimmed mean/stdev. Everything derives from the
bucket iterator:

- first/last/range/count — band's first/last non-empty bucket
  bounds and summed counts; bands are quantile fences, so the
  iteration API must make **cumulative** counts easy.
- mean/stdev (overall, per-band, trimmed) — bucket-midpoint
  weighted, accurate to rel. err; original HdrHistogram
  computes them the same way.
- exact overall mean would need a running `sum` at record
  time (one u64 add) — a hot-path candidate decided by bench
  at 0.1.0-8, alongside exact min/max.
- calibration adjustment (iiac-perf's "adjusted" column) is
  presentation-layer; stays in the consumer.

## Validation

- Exhaustive small-config tests: for tiny (g, n), walk every
  value in 0..2ⁿ, assert indices are monotone and bucket
  ranges exactly partition the domain.
- Index-parity oracle: iopsystems `histogram` uses the same
  h2 scheme, so `index_for` must match exactly.
- Quantile-parity oracle: `hdrhistogram` 7 on identical
  random streams, agreeing within equivalent-value tolerance
  (the g↔sigfig mapping is approximate).
- `no_std` proof: a check script building
  `--no-default-features` for a bare-metal target.
- Record-path bench: ns/record vs a raw array store and vs
  `hdrhistogram`. Outcome (0.1.0-8): 2.6 ns/record vs
  hdrhistogram's 4.9; exact min/max/sum would add ~28%, so
  they stay **off** the hot path (bench numbers and decision
  in chores-01).

## Deferred directions

Kept open, not designed (see TODO.md):

- Buffer-swap servicing: per-probe 1-bit "needs service soon"
  signal; a background task swaps a fresh buffer for the full
  one. Enabled by the config/storage split.
- Interval snapshot / reset; atomic concurrent recording
  (`portable-atomic`); HdrHistogram V2 wire format; `alloc`
  feature (Vec-backed storage, auto-resize).

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and goals.
- [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) — live task list and the 0.1.0 ladder.
- [`notes/chores/`](notes/chores) — chores-*.md files contain
  discussion and notes on various chores in github compatible
  markdown.

- first/last/range/count — band's first/last non-empty bucket
  bounds and summed counts; bands are quantile fences, so the
  iteration API must make **cumulative** counts easy.
- mean/stdev (overall, per-band, trimmed) — bucket-midpoint
  weighted, accurate to rel. err; original HdrHistogram
  computes them the same way.
- exact overall mean would need a running `sum` at record
  time (one u64 add) — a hot-path candidate decided by bench
  at 0.1.0-8, alongside exact min/max.
- calibration adjustment (iiac-perf's "adjusted" column) is
  presentation-layer; stays in the consumer.

## Validation

- Exhaustive small-config tests: for tiny (g, n), walk every
  value in 0..2ⁿ, assert indices are monotone and bucket
  ranges exactly partition the domain.
- Index-parity oracle: iopsystems `histogram` uses the same
  h2 scheme, so `index_for` must match exactly.
- Quantile-parity oracle: `hdrhistogram` 7 on identical
  random streams, agreeing within equivalent-value tolerance
  (the g↔sigfig mapping is approximate).
- `no_std` proof: a check script building
  `--no-default-features` for a bare-metal target.
- Record-path bench: ns/record vs a raw array store and vs
  `hdrhistogram`. Outcome (0.1.0-8): 2.6 ns/record vs
  hdrhistogram's 4.9; exact min/max/sum would add ~28%, so
  they stay **off** the hot path (bench numbers and decision
  in chores-01).

## Deferred directions

Kept open, not designed (see TODO.md):

- Buffer-swap servicing: per-probe 1-bit "needs service soon"
  signal; a background task swaps a fresh buffer for the full
  one. Enabled by the config/storage split.
- Interval snapshot / reset; atomic concurrent recording
  (`portable-atomic`); HdrHistogram V2 wire format; `alloc`
  feature (Vec-backed storage, auto-resize).

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and goals.
- [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) — live task list and the 0.1.0 ladder.
- [`notes/chores/`](notes/chores) — chores-*.md files contain
  discussion and notes on various chores in github compatible
  markdown.

# Architecture

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It records
the design choices for the crate and the 0.1.0 starting point.
Code structure sections will grow as the ladder lands; the
choices below are the founding state (2026-07-21, pre-scaffold).

## Overview

The crate is a `no_std`, no-alloc reimplementation of the
HdrHistogram log-linear bucketing scheme (Gil Tene et al.,
[HdrHistogram](https://github.com/HdrHistogram/HdrHistogram)),
sized and shaped for tprobe-class continuous monitoring: tens
to hundreds of probes per process, each recording tick-counter
deltas run-to-completion, analyzed at the end of the run.

- The one hot operation is `record(value)`: find the bucket
  (clz + shift), saturating-increment its counter. No floats,
  no allocation, no panics, no data-dependent work.
- Everything else — quantiles, merge, iteration — is
  O(buckets), `no_std`-capable, and off the measured path.
- Lifecycle for 0.1.0 is run-to-completion: create, record,
  analyze at end. No snapshot/swap/reset API yet (see
  [Deferred directions](#deferred-directions)).

## Parameterization (h2)

The h2 restatement of HdrHistogram's bucketing — borrowed from
iopsystems' [histogram](https://github.com/iopsystems/histogram)
crate, written up at [h2histogram.org](https://h2histogram.org/)
— replaces `(lowest, highest, significant_figures)` with
two powers, chosen per instance at runtime:

- `grouping_power` (g) — each power-of-two range is split
  into 2ᵍ linear sub-buckets; relative value error ≤ 2⁻ᵍ.
- `max_value_power` (n) — max trackable value is 2ⁿ−1;
  over-range values clamp into the top bucket (for lateness
  detection the overflow *is* the signal).
- Values below 2ᵍ⁺¹ are recorded exactly.
- `buckets = (n − g + 1) · 2ᵍ`.

## Storage: config / counts split

The load-bearing structural choice: config and counts storage
are separate, and the core histogram type *borrows* its counts.

- `Config` — the two powers plus derived index math; `const fn`
  so callers can size storage at compile time.
- `Histogram<'a, C>` — config + `&'a mut [C]` counts; no
  derived state (totals are summed at read time, keeping the
  record path minimal). The caller owns the memory: a static
  buffer, a stack array, or (with `std`) a heap slice.
- `HistogramArray<const N, C>` — owned inline `[C; N]`
  convenience wrapper with a size check against
  `Config::total_buckets()`. Rust's stable const generics
  can't derive N from (g, n) directly (`generic_const_exprs`
  is unstable), so N is explicit and checked.
- Why borrowing is the default: the future buffer-swap model —
  a background task handing a probe a fresh zeroed slice and
  taking the full one — needs detachable storage. Keeping
  config and counts unfused now means that model lands later
  without reshaping the type.

## Counts

- Counter type is generic (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`) with
  **u32 the default**; increments saturate — no panic, no
  wraparound lies.
- We think u32 is the right monitoring default because a
  deployment that watches stability will bound per-histogram
  totals (by run length now, by snapshot interval later);
  u64 is for run-forever accumulation and doubles the
  footprint for it.

## Feature map

- Core: `#![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)]`,
  no dependencies.
- `std` (default): convenience only — the demo example, the
  stdout side of reporting, and any std-needing helpers.
- Band-table reporting **is** in this crate, as of 0.1.3, and
  is `no_std`: the modules build report *structures*
  (`bands`, `stats`, `table`) and render into any
  `core::fmt::Write` (`report`); only writing to stdout needs
  `std`. This reverses the founding decision to leave
  reporting to tprobe — see
  [Readout requirements](#readout-requirements-band-tables).
- Dev-dependencies (tests/benches only): iopsystems
  `histogram` and `hdrhistogram` 7 as correctness oracles,
  plus a bench harness.

## API sketch (provisional)

```rust
pub struct Config { /* grouping_power, max_value_power */ }
impl Config {
    pub const fn new(g: u8, n: u8) -> Result<Config, Error>;
    pub const fn total_buckets(&self) -> usize;
    pub const fn index_for(&self, value: u64) -> usize;
    pub const fn value_range(&self, index: usize) -> (u64, u64);
}

pub struct Histogram<'a, C: Counter = u32> {
    // config, counts: &'a mut [C]
}
impl<C: Counter> Histogram<'_, C> {
    pub fn record(&mut self, value: u64);
    pub fn record_n(&mut self, value: u64, count: u64);
    pub fn quantile(&self, q: f64) -> Option<u64>;
    pub fn merge_from(&mut self, other: &Self) -> Result<(), Error>;
    pub fn buckets(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Bucket>;
}

pub struct HistogramArray<const N: usize, C: Counter = u32> {
    // owned [C; N], checked against Config::total_buckets()
}
```

## Size tradeoffs

`bytes = (n − g + 1) · 2ᵍ · sizeof(C)` + ~48 B header
(min/max/config if the hot-path extras are adopted).

What a max value of 2ⁿ ticks spans in time depends on the tick
source's rate:

| n  | 100 Hz  | 1 kHz   | 3 GHz   |
|----|---------|---------|---------|
| 16 | ~11 min | ~66 s   | ~22 µs  |
| 20 | ~2.9 h  | ~17 min | ~350 µs |
| 24 | ~1.9 d  | ~4.7 h  | ~5.6 ms |
| 32 | ~1.4 y  | ~50 d   | ~1.4 s  |
| 36 | ~22 y   | ~2.2 y  | ~23 s   |
| 40 | ~349 y  | ~35 y   | ~6 min  |

A slow tick source therefore wants a much smaller n, which
shrinks the footprint directly: e.g. a 1 kHz tick watching
1 ms..65 s lateness needs only n = 16 — at g = 4/u32 that is
(16−4+1)·2⁴ = 208 buckets ≈ 832 B per probe.

| g  | n  | C   | rel. err | buckets | bytes   |
|----|----|-----|----------|---------|---------|
| 2  | 32 | u16 | 25%      | 124     | ~250 B  |
| 4  | 36 | u32 | 6.3%     | 528     | ~2.1 KB |
| 6  | 40 | u32 | 1.6%     | 2,240   | ~9 KB   |
| 7  | 40 | u32 | 0.8%     | 4,352   | ~17 KB  |
| 7  | 40 | u64 | 0.8%     | 4,352   | ~34 KB  |
| 10 | 40 | u32 | 0.1%     | 31,744  | ~124 KB |

- rel. err is the value-quantization bound 2⁻ᵍ: each
  power-of-two range holds 2ᵍ equal-width buckets, so a
  bucket spans ≤ 2⁻ᵍ of the values in it. Counts are exact;
  only the value axis is quantized — any value read back
  (quantile, boundary) is within 2⁻ᵍ of the recorded one.
  Values below 2ᵍ⁺¹ are exact (width-1 buckets).
  - 2⁻ᵍ is the full uncertainty band: reporting a bucket
    endpoint gives one-sided error up to 2⁻ᵍ; reporting the
    midpoint halves it to ±2⁻ᵍ⁄2. Which convention
    `quantile()` uses is decided at 0.1.0-5 (oracle parity
    with `hdrhistogram`'s upper-bound convention weighs in).
- g=7/u32 is parity with `hdrhistogram` at 2 significant
  figures; g=10 approximates 3.
- We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot: error is on
  the *value* axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× does
  not need 2-sigfig fidelity.
- Fleet math: 100 probes at g=6/n=40/u32 ≈ 900 KB per
  process; double it if a snapshot model later
  double-buffers each probe.

## Readout requirements (band tables)

The consumer-side bar is iiac-perf's band table: z/p/n quantile
bands, each showing first/last/range/count/mean, plus overall
and quantile-trimmed mean/stdev. As of 0.1.3 that bar is met
*in this crate* rather than left to consumers, because the
duplication the original decision meant to avoid turned out to
run the other way: the same accumulate-then-render loop existed
four times (h2demo plus iiac-perf's `harness.rs`,
`band_table.rs`, `probe.rs`), none shared. One `no_std`
implementation at the bottom of the stack is what removes it.

Everything derives from the bucket iterator:

- first/last/range/count — band's first/last non-empty bucket
  bounds and summed counts; bands are quantile fences, so the
  iteration API must make **cumulative** counts easy.
- mean/stdev (overall, per-band, trimmed) — bucket-midpoint
  weighted, accurate to rel. err; original HdrHistogram
  computes them the same way.
- exact overall mean would need a running `sum` at record
  time (one u64 add) — a hot-path candidate decided by bench
  at 0.1.0-8, alongside exact min/max.
- calibration adjustment (iiac-perf's "adjusted" column) is
  presentation-layer; stays in the consumer.

## Validation

- Exhaustive small-config tests: for tiny (g, n), walk every
  value in 0..2ⁿ, assert indices are monotone and bucket
  ranges exactly partition the domain.
- Index-parity oracle: iopsystems `histogram` uses the same
  h2 scheme, so `index_for` must match exactly.
- Quantile-parity oracle: `hdrhistogram` 7 on identical
  random streams, agreeing within equivalent-value tolerance
  (the g↔sigfig mapping is approximate).
- `no_std` proof: a check script building
  `--no-default-features` for a bare-metal target.
- Record-path bench: ns/record vs a raw array store and vs
  `hdrhistogram`. Outcome (0.1.0-8): 2.6 ns/record vs
  hdrhistogram's 4.9; exact min/max/sum would add ~28%, so
  they stay **off** the hot path (bench numbers and decision
  in chores-01).

## Deferred directions

Kept open, not designed (see TODO.md):

- Buffer-swap servicing: per-probe 1-bit "needs service soon"
  signal; a background task swaps a fresh buffer for the full
  one. Enabled by the config/storage split.
- Interval snapshot / reset; atomic concurrent recording
  (`portable-atomic`); HdrHistogram V2 wire format; `alloc`
  feature (Vec-backed storage, auto-resize).

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and goals.
- [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) — live task list and the 0.1.0 ladder.
- [`notes/chores/`](notes/chores) — chores-*.md files contain
  discussion and notes on various chores in github compatible
  markdown.

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (current)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules

ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo

content.text:
Preparation work is complete and the cargo cycle is green (fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, 24 unit + 3 oracle + 1 doctest, install). Ready to commit.

**What changed**

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version-of-record `0.1.2` → `0.1.3-0`.
- `TODO.md`
  - `## In Progress` now carries the cycle: bold title, the problem statement (band-table capability stranded in the demo; four unshared copies of the loop; duplicated dev literals), the `no_std`-structures framing, and the 10-rung ladder with `-0` marked `(done)`.
  - New `## Todo` #3, "iiac-perf adoption", peer to the tprobe entry.
  - New ref `[5]` → the new chores section.
- `notes/chores/chores-01.md` — opened `## feat: no_std band report modules` with an empty `Commits:` line, the intro, and three design subsections written now because they're decisions rather than outcomes: **Reporting moves into the crate** (why the founding decision reverses — the duplication ran the opposite direction from what it assumed), **Constraints from staying no_std** (sqrt/floor/powi and the alloc-free rendering), **Numeric fix carried along** (the two-pass variance). Plus an empty `### As-built ladder` for the Work commits to append to.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - Feature map: the "band-table reporting stays in tprobe" bullet is replaced by the reversal, scoped to `no_std` structures with only stdout behind `std`.
  - Readout requirements: records that the bar is now met in-crate, and why.
  - The mean/stdev bullet gains the variance-vs-stdev and two-pass-variance decisions.

**One thing I changed that you didn't ask for:** `ARCHITECTURE.md` had two `## Validation` headings — an empty one immediately above `## Readout requirements` (a stray) and the real one below it. I dropped the empty one while editing that region. Say the word and I'll put it back.

**Note on the ladder refs:** every rung carries a literal `[[N]]` placeholder per the `TODO.md` template. They render as unresolved until backfilled, which per the protocol happens one push after each step lands.

```
Preparation for the band report cycle: promote the band-table
capability out of examples/h2demo.rs into no_std crate modules,
and collapse the dev-side duplication across test, bench, and
demo. Sets the version-of-record, the ladder, and the chores
section, and records the founding decision this reverses.

    entry; ref [5] to the new chores section

  - feature map: reverses "band-table reporting stays in
    tprobe" — reporting is in-crate and no_std, building
    report structures, with only stdout behind std
  - readout requirements: records that the bar is now met
    here, and that the duplication ran opposite to what the
    original decision assumed
  - mean/stdev: core has no sqrt, so the structure carries
    variance and stdev() is offered where a sqrt exists;
    variance accumulated two-pass rather than sumsq/n - mean^2

    read-time total", now permanent on main; adds the [12]

```

The `ochid:` trailer is omitted deliberately — `vc-x1 push` injects it.

May I commit this?

content.content:
vc-x1 0.74.0-0

Commit both repos, push the work repo's BOOKMARK, and
squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.

Collapses the manual commit-push-publish ceremony into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (tracking / bot-published / sync checks —
no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing)
→ review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-work → commit-bot (skipped if clean) → bookmark-set
(work repo → <bookmark>, bot repo → main) → push-work →
squash-push-bot. Failures in commit-work / commit-bot /
bookmark-set roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-work.
After push-work succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (positional form of
          `--bookmark`). The bot repo always advances `main`.
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

Options:

content.content:
Possible values:
          - preflight:       Verify bookmark tracking, the bot-published
            invariant, and sync state
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-work:     Commit the work repo
          - commit-bot:      Commit the `.claude` bot repo (skipped if empty)
          - bookmark-set:    Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: work →
            `<bookmark>`, bot → `main`
          - push-work:       `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - squash-push-bot: In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session
            writes + push `main`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-squash-push
          Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied
          in non-interactive contexts

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Undone — the five files are back in the working copy, `@` is a child of `main`, and the trailer-less commit is gone. Bookmark `refactor-common-modules` created at the `main` tip (`9a79e0eb`), so `bookmark-set` will advance it onto the new commit.

Pushing now with the approved title and body — `vc-x1 push` handles both repos and injects the `ochid:` trailers each way. Since the review and description gates already happened here in conversation, I'm passing `--title`/`--body`/`--yes` so it doesn't block on prompts I can't answer.

If preflight rejects the brand-new untracked bookmark, it rolls both repos back before any remote write, and I'll report that rather than retry.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules

String: /// Record 1,000,000 deterministic synthetic latency-tick samples
/// (seed 42) into `h`.
fn record_samples(h: &mut Histogram<'_, u32>) {
    let mut rng = SplitMix64(42);
    let max_value = h.config().max_value();
    for _ in 0..1_000_000u64 {
        let base = 50 + (rng.next() % 100);
        let v = match rng.next() % 1000 {
            0 => base * (1 + rng.next() % 100),
            1..=9 => base * (1 + rng.next() % 100),
            _ => base,
        }
        .clamp(1, max_value);
        h.record(v);
    }
}</tool_use_error>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (current)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules

### As-built ladder

input.new_string:
### As-built ladder

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup — version-of-record
  to 0.1.3-0, the ladder into `## In Progress`, this chores section
  opened with its design subsections, the ARCHITECTURE.md reversal,
  and the iiac-perf adoption todo. Cycle runs on branch
  `refactor-common-modules`.
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo — one
  `dev/` module (consts, rng, stream) behind a single `#[path]`
  include per consumer, plus the same const-derivation applied to
  the `src/` unit tests:
  - `SplitMix64` went from three verbatim copies to one, and the
    heavy-tailed stream from three spellings to one `HeavyTailed`
    iterator. The stream's `next()` makes the same PRNG calls in
    the same order as the copies it replaces, so every consumer's
    values are unchanged — the demo's output is byte-identical.
  - The config, seed, oracle precision, and stream shape are
    `dev/consts.rs` constants. `CFG` is a `const Config`, so
    `BUCKETS` sizes storage at compile time and the printed
    `g=…  n=…` header is generated from the same powers the
    histogram is built from rather than restating them.
  - The `hdrhistogram` side of both the bench and the parity test
    is now built from `HDR_SIGFIG`, and the parity tolerance is
    computed from the two precisions (`2^-g + 10^-sigfig`) rather
    than carrying a hardcoded `0.01`. That pair is the one that
    must not drift: the oracle is only meaningful if both sides
    describe the same precision.
  - `src/` unit tests paired a `Config::new(g, n)` with a
    hand-computed array length (`[0u32; 28]`, `HistogramArray<80>`).
    Each test now declares `const C` / `const N = C.total_buckets()`
    via a `const fn cfg`, so a changed config cannot leave a stale
    length behind. The deliberately-wrong lengths became `N - 1`,
    keeping them wrong by construction.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-0"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]

content.text:
Work complete for `0.1.3-1`. The full cargo cycle passed: fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, all tests (24 unit + 3 oracle + 1 doctest), no_std build across four bare-metal targets, install. The demo output is byte-identical to the pre-refactor baseline.

**What changed**

New `dev/` module, included by each consumer with a single `#[path = "../dev/mod.rs"] mod dev;`:
- `dev/mod.rs` — re-exports; `#![allow(dead_code)]` since each consumer uses only part.
- `dev/consts.rs` — `SEED`, `GROUPING_POWER`/`MAX_VALUE_POWER`, `const CFG: Config`, `BUCKETS`, `HDR_SIGFIG`, and the six stream-shape constants.
- `dev/rng.rs` — the one `SplitMix64`.
- `dev/stream.rs` — `HeavyTailed`, an `Iterator` yielding the same values in the same PRNG-call order as the three copies it replaces.

Consumers rewired:
- `benches/record.rs` — stream from `HeavyTailed`, storage as `[_; BUCKETS]`, `hdrhistogram` built from `HDR_SIGFIG`, header generated from the powers.
- `examples/h2demo.rs` — `SplitMix64` + `record_samples`'s hand-rolled loop gone; storage/header from consts; `SAMPLES` const.
- `tests/oracle.rs` — `SplitMix64`/stream from `dev/`; `PARITY_CONFIGS`/`PARITY_SEED`/sample counts as consts; parity tolerance computed as `2^-g + 10^-sigfig` instead of a hardcoded `0.01`.

`src/` test-literal cleanup (the folded-in part): `analysis.rs`, `array.rs`, `histogram.rs` — each test's `Config::new(g,n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` becomes `const C` + `const N = C.total_buckets()` via a local `const fn cfg`; wrong-length cases became `N - 1`.

Notes: `TODO.md` `-1` marked `(done)`; chores As-built rung appended.

Version-of-record bumped to `0.1.3-1` (`Cargo.toml` + `Cargo.lock`).

Work complete. Please review — I'll write the commit description once you're ready.

content.text:
I have the full inventory. Before executing, two identifiers are genuine exceptions I'll **keep and flag** rather than rename, because the "can't find the type in a diff" rationale doesn't apply to them and Rust convention is overwhelming:

- **`'a`** — a lifetime label, not a value/type name; there's no type to look up.
- **`N`** — the const-generic array length in `HistogramArray<const N: usize, …>`; it's always `usize` and `[T; N]` is Rust's own array syntax.

Everything else single-char gets a descriptive name. Tell me if you want `'a` → `'counts` and `N` → `CAP` too and I'll fold them in.

Also settling the contested public-API names: `q` → `fraction` (can't be `quantile` — clashes with the method), and the count arg `n` in `record_n`/`record_into` → `count`. Starting the renames.

Done — the single-character rename is folded into this `0.1.3-1` commit alongside the dev-module refactor. Full cargo cycle passed: fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, all tests (24 unit + 3 oracle + 1 doctest), no_std build across four targets, install. Demo output still byte-identical.

**The rename**

- **`Counter` generic `C` → `Cnt`** everywhere (structs, impls, free functions, both histogram types and the iterator). This was your original target — `counts: &'a [C]` now reads `counts: &'a [Cnt]`.
- **Counter locals**: `c` → `cnt`; merge's `d`/`s` → `dst_cnt`/`src_cnt`; `count_at`'s closure `|c|` → `|cnt|`.
- **Public API params**: quantile's `q` → `fraction` (can't be `quantile` — method clash); `record_n`/`record_into`'s count `n` → `count`; `Counter::from_u64_sat`'s `v` → `value`.
- **Index-math locals** (`config.rs`): `v` → `clamped`, `m` → `offset`.
- **Test / bench / dev bindings**: `cfg(g, n)` → `cfg(grouping_power, max_value_power)`; per-test `const C`/`N` → `CFG`/`BUCKETS` (or `EXACT_*`/`TINY_*`/`OTHER_*` where a distinct config avoids shadowing); `h` → `hist`; loop `v`/`i`/`k`/`p` → `value`/`index`/`exp`/`power_of_two`; bench closure `f` → `run`, `t0` → `start`, `io`/`e` → `iop`/`error`; splitmix's `z` → `mixed`.

**Two kept, by the reasoning below** — say the word and I'll change them:
- **`'a`** — a lifetime label, no type to look up.
- **`N`** — the const-generic array length in `HistogramArray<const N: usize, …>`; always `usize`, and `[T; N]` is Rust's own array syntax.

**Left alone deliberately:** the `g=`/`n=`/`v=`/`q=` labels *inside* assert format strings (h2 output notation a reader sees on failure), and `examples/h2demo.rs`'s internals — it's rewritten onto the library report path in `0.1.3-7`, so renaming now is throwaway. Both noted in the chores rung.

Work complete. Please review — I'll write the commit description when you're ready.

Good — `'a` stays, and `N` → `LEN` (uppercase, since it's a const param). No changes yet; here's the discussion.

## The reorder you sketched won't compile

Your proposed shape:

```rust
pub struct HistogramArray<CntType: CounterType = u32, const LEN: usize> {
```

hits a hard Rust rule: **a generic parameter with a default must be trailing** — nothing without a default may follow it. So a defaulted type param (`= u32`) cannot come before a non-defaulted const param (`const LEN: usize`). rustc rejects it with exactly that message. That constraint is *why* the current code is `<const N: usize, Cnt: Counter = u32>` — const first, defaulted type last. The order isn't a style choice; it's forced by the `= u32` default.

There are three legal ways to arrange it, with real tradeoffs:

- **`<const LEN: usize, Cnt: Counter = u32>`** (current order, just `N`→`LEN`). Keeps the `= u32` default. Caller writes `HistogramArray::<28>` and gets `Cnt = u32` for free — the length is the thing you almost always specify, so it's positionally first. This is the ergonomic winner.
- **`<Cnt: Counter, const LEN: usize>`** (type first, your preferred reading order). Legal, but only because neither has a default — so you **lose** `= u32`. Every caller must now write `HistogramArray::<u32, 28>`. Worse ergonomics for the common case.
- **`<Cnt: Counter = u32, const LEN: usize = 0>`** (type first, defaults on both). Legal, but `LEN = 0` is a nonsense default (a zero-bucket histogram), and `HistogramArray::<u32>` would silently pick it. A footgun; I'd reject this.

## The key point: the field already reads the way you want

The thing you're reacting to — type-before-length — is really about the **field**, and the field's order is independent of the generic-parameter-list order:

```rust
pub struct HistogramArray<const LEN: usize, Cnt: Counter = u32> {
    config: Config,
    counts: [Cnt; LEN],   // reads [type; length] regardless of the <...> order
}
```

`[Cnt; LEN]` is already `[type; length]`, matching Rust's own array syntax `[T; N]`. So you get the readable form in the field even though the `<...>` list is const-first for the default rule.

## Naming: `Cnt` vs `CntType`, `Counter` vs `CounterType`

My lean:

- **Keep the trait `Counter`.** `Cnt: Counter` reads as "a counter type that implements Counter." Renaming to `CounterType` is a public-trait rename for little gain, and `Counter` is already the natural noun.
- **`Cnt` over `CntType`.** `Cnt` starts with a capital and sits in type position (`[Cnt; LEN]`, `<Cnt: Counter>`), so a reader already sees it's a type — the diff-readability problem you raised is solved by it being PascalCase, not lowercase like the old `C`… wait, `C` was also capital. The real fix was length, not case. `CntType` is unambiguous but the `Type` suffix is redundant with the `: Counter` bound right next to it. I'd call it a wash and slightly prefer `Cnt`, but `CntType` is fine if you like the explicitness.

## My recommendation

```rust
pub struct HistogramArray<const LEN: usize, Cnt: Counter = u32> {
    config: Config,
    counts: [Cnt; LEN],
}
```

`N` → `LEN`, keep `Cnt`/`Counter`, keep the order (forced by the default and best for `::<28>` callers). If you specifically want the type param named first in the `<...>` list, the only legal way costs the `u32` default — a real API regression I'd advise against.

**The plain version:** you wanted the counter *type* written before the array *length*. In the actual field (`[Cnt; LEN]`) it already is. In the angle-bracket list, though, Rust forces the length first, because the counter type has a default value (`u32`) and Rust makes anything with a default go last. Trying to flip them means giving up that `u32` default, so callers would have to spell out the type every time. Best to just rename `N` to `LEN` and leave the order alone.

On to it. First my read on `CntT` / `CounterT`, then I'll make the `N` → `LEN` change.

## `CntT` / `CounterT`: not an improvement

The trailing bare `T` is itself a lone cryptic letter — the exact thing you objected to in `C`. `CntT` makes a reader parse "Cnt" + a dangling "T" that they have to decode as "Type." And I'd keep the trait as **`Counter`**: a trait already names a type-level capability, so `CounterT` just bolts a mystery letter onto a word that already reads well.

If the goal is an explicit "this is a type" cue, the readable form is the full suffix, not the abbreviation:

- `CntType: Counter` — unambiguous, no lone letters. Slightly verbose, and the `Type` is a touch redundant next to the `: Counter` bound sitting right beside it.
- `Cnt: Counter` — my pick. It's PascalCase in type position (`[Cnt; LEN]`, `<Cnt: Counter>`), so it already reads as a type; the problem with `C` was its *length*, not its case.

Ranking: `Cnt: Counter` ≥ `CntType: Counter` > `CntT: CounterT`. I'd leave it `Cnt`, but `CntType` is the one to pick if you want the explicit cue. Tell me which and I'll apply it — for now leaving the param name as `Cnt` and making only the `N` → `LEN` change you asked for.

message.content:
Here is another gotcha for me, I'm looking at this code in analysis.rs:
pub struct Buckets<'a, Cnt: Counter> {
    config: Config,
    counts: &'a [Cnt],
    index: usize,
    cumulative: u64,
}

What is Counter in <'a, Cnt: Counter>? A Trait with a `fn to_u64(self) -> u64;` define in  counter.rs OMG!

content.text:
Yes — you've got it exactly right. `Counter` is a **trait** (defined in `counter.rs`), and `Cnt: Counter` is a *trait bound*: it reads "`Cnt` is a type parameter, and it must be a type that implements `Counter`."

Breaking the declaration `pub struct Buckets<'a, Cnt: Counter>` into its three pieces:

- `'a` — a lifetime (the borrow of the counts slice).
- `Cnt` — a generic **type** parameter: a placeholder for "whatever integer the counts are stored as."
- `: Counter` — the **bound** constraining `Cnt`: it may only be a type that implements the `Counter` trait.

`Counter` (in `counter.rs`) is implemented for `u8`, `u16`, `u32`, `u64`. So `Cnt` is *one of those four*, and the struct — plus every method on it — is written once and works for all four, because the trait gives the generic code a shared vocabulary: `to_u64`, `from_u64_sat`, `sat_add`. The generic code never needs to know *which* integer it is; it just calls those trait methods.

## The actual gotcha you hit

**Rust names traits and structs identically — both PascalCase.** `Counter` (a trait) and `Config` (a struct) look the same at a glance. Nothing in the *name* tells you which is which; only the **position** does:

- `counts: &'a [Cnt]` — `Cnt` here is in *type* position (an element type). It's a type.
- `config: Config` — `Config` in type position. A struct (a concrete type).
- `Cnt: Counter` — after the `:` in a generic list, the right side is a *bound*. A trait.
- `-> u64` — a concrete type.

So the rule that recovers it without an editor: **`X: Y` inside `<…>` means `Y` is a trait.** That's the grammar cue that replaces the hover. (It bites the other way too — a bare `Config` and a bare `Counter` are indistinguishable as strings; you have to know one's a `struct` and one's a `trait` from their definitions.)

**The plain version:** `Counter` is a rulebook (a "trait") that says "a type usable as a bucket count must be able to do these things: widen to u64, narrow back, add-and-saturate." `u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64` each follow that rulebook. `Cnt: Counter` means "let `Cnt` be any type that follows the Counter rulebook," so one copy of the code serves all four integer widths. The confusing part is only that Rust spells trait names and struct names the same way — you tell them apart by *where* they sit, not by how they look.

No changes here — just answering. If it'd help, I could add a one-line note to the `Counter` trait's or `Buckets`' doc comment spelling out "the counts' integer type (u8–u64)," but the doc on `counter.rs` already covers it.

**Body:**
```
Collapse the duplicated test/bench/demo scaffolding into one
`dev/` module, and rename single-character identifiers across
the crate to descriptive ones so a diff or plain-text view
carries the meaning an editor's hover would.

- dev/mod.rs, dev/consts.rs, dev/rng.rs, dev/stream.rs: new
  shared module (one `#[path]` include per consumer). Holds the
  single `SplitMix64`, the `HeavyTailed` stream, and the config
  / seed / oracle-precision consts; stream-shape consts live in
  stream.rs (its only reader).
- src/counter.rs: `Counter` generic `C` → `Cnt`; trait method
  and macro `v`/`$t` → `value`/`$ty`.
- src/config.rs: index-math locals `v`/`m` → `clamped`/`offset`;
  test bindings `g`/`n`/`c`/`v` → full names.
- src/histogram.rs: generic `C` → `Cnt`; count arg `n` →
  `count`; locals `c` → `cnt`; per-test configs `C`/`N` →
  `CFG`/`BUCKETS` (or `EXACT_*`/`TINY_*`).
- src/array.rs: generic `C` → `Cnt`; const-generic length
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules

toolUseResult.newString: version = "0.1.3-2"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-1"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]
name = "record"
harness = false

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
4	contains near term tasks with a short description and
5	uses links or reference links for more details.
6	
7	## In Progress
8	
9	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
10	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
11	by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
12	"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
13	replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
14	the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
15	`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
16	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
17	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
18	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation
20	
21	At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
22	`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
23	(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).
24	
25	**feat: no_std band report modules**
26	
27	The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
28	`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
29	`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
30	mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
31	more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
32	`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
33	the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
34	`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
35	silently diverges from the others.
36	
37	Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
38	*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
39	reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
40	decision — see [[5]].
41	
42	- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
43	  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
44	    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
45	- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
46	  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
47	    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
48	    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
49	    literals
50	  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
51	    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
52	    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
53	- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels (current)
54	  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
55	    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
56	    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
57	- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
58	  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
59	    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
60	    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
61	- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
62	  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
63	    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
64	    where a `sqrt` exists
65	- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
66	  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
67	    over `Buckets`
68	- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
69	  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
70	    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
71	- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
72	  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
73	    at matching ladder depths
74	- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
75	  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
76	    ladder as a visible change
77	- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
78	  - close-out and validation
79	
80	## Todo
81	
82	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
83	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
84	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
85	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
86	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
87	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
88	 number. Long-tail entries
89	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
90	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
91	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
92	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
93	
94	1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
95	   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
96	   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
97	   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
98	   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
99	   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
100	2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
101	   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
102	   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
103	   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
104	3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
105	   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
106	   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
107	   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
108	   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
109	   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
110	   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
111	   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
112	   first. See [[5]].
113	
114	## Ideas
115	
116	- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
117	  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
118	- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
119	- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
120	  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
121	- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
122	
123	## Bugs
124	
125	See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).
126	
127	## Done
128	
129	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
130	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.
131	
132	- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
133	- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
134	- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]
