144	[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"

1	# Chores-01
2	
3	Chores-XX files use [Prose form](../../AGENTS.md#prose-form). They
4	contain discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible
5	markdown. There is also a [TODO.md](../../TODO.md) file that tracks
6	tasks and in general there should be a chore section for each task
7	with the why and how this task will be completed.
8	
9	Each task's `##` header is its commit title (see
10	[Conventional-commit shape](../../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit)),
11	with a `Commits:` line first under it — empty until backfilled —
12	then the narrative: conceptual bullets, never a per-file edit
13	list (see
14	[Chores conventions](../../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions)).
15	
16	## feat: no_std h2 histogram core
17	
18	Commits: [[10]]
19	
20	A `no_std`, no-alloc, HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram
21	crate, to become tprobe's core recording structure. tprobe's
22	chores-01 (its 0.1.0-8 decision) surveyed crates.io and found
23	no fit — `hdrhistogram` 7.5.4 is std-only, iopsystems
24	`histogram` is heap-backed, and the `no_std` options have the
25	wrong bucketing — and decided to hand-roll; this repo is that
26	hand-roll promoted to its own crate. Cycle runs on branch
27	`0.1.0-no-std-hdrhistogram` with a non-ff merge close-out
28	(manual jj steps until vc-x1 grows the capability, after its
29	current refactoring).
30	
31	### Founding discussion (2026-07-21)
32	
33	The founding conversation (feasibility → sizing → plan) landed
34	these decisions; sizing detail and the API sketch live in
35	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md), not restated here.
36	
37	- Feasibility: the record path is clz + shift + increment —
38	  integer-only, O(1), trivially `no_std`. The std-bound parts
39	  of existing implementations (dynamic sizing, auto-resize,
40	  compressed wire format, f64 conveniences) are separable and
41	  deferred or feature-gated.
42	- h2 parameterization `(grouping_power, max_value_power)`
43	  over the original `(lowest, highest, sigfigs)` — same
44	  bucketing, cleaner integer math, `const fn`-friendly
45	  bucket-count formula. Config chosen per probe instance.
46	- Config / counts-storage split, core borrowing
47	  `&mut [Count]` — the load-bearing choice; keeps the
48	  deferred buffer-swap model open without reshaping the type.
49	- u32 saturating counts as default. We think monitoring
50	  bounds per-histogram totals (run length now, snapshot
51	  interval later), so u64 is reserved for run-forever
52	  accumulation.
53	- Lifecycle for 0.1.0: one histogram per probe,
54	  run-to-completion, analysis at the end. No snapshot /
55	  reset / swap API yet.
56	- Validation via two oracles: iopsystems `histogram` for
57	  exact index parity (same scheme), `hdrhistogram` 7 for
58	  quantile parity within equivalent-value tolerance; plus
59	  exhaustive small-config walks and a `no_std` target build.
60	- Demo ships as `examples/h2demo.rs`, installable via
61	  `cargo install --path . --example h2demo` (cargo installs
62	  example targets with `--example`).
63	- Readout bar: iiac-perf's band table (z/p/n bands,
64	  first/last/range/count/mean, trimmed mean/stdev) must be
65	  derivable from the bucket iterator — drives cumulative
66	  counts in the iteration API; `h2demo` prints that table.
67	- Parked open questions: hot-path extras — exact min/max and
68	  running sum for exact mean — decided on bench data at
69	  0.1.0-8; f64-vs-integer quantile input (decide at
70	  0.1.0-5); crate rename before any publish.
71	
72	### Sizing analysis
73	
74	A sample costs zero bytes — footprint is fixed at creation:
75	`bytes = (n−g+1)·2ᵍ·sizeof(C)`, ~250 B to ~250 KB across
76	realistic configs (table in
77	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md#size-tradeoffs)).
78	We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot — error is on
79	the value axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× doesn't
80	need 2-sigfig fidelity — putting a 100-probe process near
81	1 MB, noise on a host and shrinkable for embedded.
82	
83	### Deferred: buffer-swap servicing model
84	
85	The intended growth path once run-to-completion is limiting:
86	each probe raises a 1-bit "needs service soon" signal (a
87	watermark on total count or a near-saturated bucket); a
88	background task hands the probe a fresh zeroed counts buffer
89	and takes the full one for analysis. No hard decisions made —
90	the config/storage split is the only accommodation. Watermark
91	semantics, signal transport, and hand-off protocol are all
92	open; tracked as the "Buffer-swap servicing model" Todo.
93	
94	### Record-cost bench and the hot-path-extras decision
95	
96	`benches/record.rs` (hand-rolled, harness=false; g=7 n=30,
97	8M-value heavy-tailed stream, best of 3, 3900X, unpinned —
98	indicative, not a lab record):
99	
100	- raw streaming store 0.87 ns/record
101	- histogram u32 2.57 ns/record
102	- histogram u32 + min/max/sum extras 3.29 ns/record
103	- hdrhistogram (2 sigfig) 4.95 ns/record
104	
105	**Decision: extras stay off the hot path.** Exact min/max and
106	running sum would cost ~+28% per record; band tables already
107	get bucket-resolution first/last and midpoint means from the
108	iterator (h2demo proves the readout), so the default record
109	path keeps only the bucket increment + total. Revisit only if
110	a consumer needs exact mean/min/max — that would be an opt-in
111	wrapper, not a change to `record`. We think the histogram-vs-
112	raw-store gap (2.6 vs 0.9 ns) overstates the field cost: the
113	raw store here is cache-warm and the histogram price buys a
114	fixed footprint, per tprobe's round-3 verdict.
115	
116	### As-built ladder
117	
118	- [[1]] 0.1.0-0 chore: h2 histogram plan capture — the
119	  founding-conversation capture: this section, README goals,
120	  ARCHITECTURE + size table, TODO ladder;
121	  version.toml → 0.1.0-0.
122	- [[2]] 0.1.0-1 chore: scaffold h2 histogram crate —
123	  CargoRust.toml renamed to Cargo.toml (name
124	  `histogram-no-std`, `std` feature, version-of-record moves
125	  here at 0.1.0-1); version.toml retired; src/lib.rs
126	  skeleton with module doc + `no_std` gate.
127	- [[3]] 0.1.0-2 feat: h2 histogram config and index math —
128	  `Config` (validated powers, `const fn` bucket count /
129	  value→index / index→range), `Error`; exhaustive
130	  small-config walk proves indices monotone and ranges an
131	  exact partition; over-range clamps to top bucket, n=64
132	  edge covered.
133	- [[4]] 0.1.0-3 feat: h2 histogram record path — `Counter`
134	  trait (u8..u64, saturating, u32 default) and the
135	  borrowed-storage `Histogram`:
136	  - `new` checks storage length and *recomputes* `total`
137	    from the counts, so a slice handed back by a future
138	    swap servicer rebinds consistently;
139	  - `record`/`record_n` never panic (`get_mut` guarded by
140	    the tested index invariant); `into_counts` releases the
141	    borrow — the swap hand-off shape.
142	- [[5]] 0.1.0-4 feat: h2 histogram owned-array wrapper —
143	  `HistogramArray<N, C>` owns `[C; N]`; N is explicit
144	  (stable Rust can't derive it from Config powers) and
145	  checked in `new`; record path shared with the borrowed
146	  type via `record_into`; `as_histogram()` gives one
147	  analysis surface for both storage shapes (carries the
148	  crate's first non-test `unwrap`, `// OK:` justified —
149	  constructor-checked invariant).
150	- [[6]] 0.1.0-5 feat: h2 histogram quantile merge and iter —
151	  `analysis` module shared by both storage shapes: `Bucket` /
152	  `Buckets` iterator carrying **cumulative** counts (the
153	  band-table requirement), `quantile` (upper-bound
154	  convention — hdrhistogram's highest-equivalent — chosen
155	  for oracle parity; integer ceil, no std float intrinsics),
156	  `merge_from` (saturating, `ConfigMismatch` on unequal
157	  configs). Decision recorded: f64 quantile input kept.
158	- [[7]] 0.1.0-6 test: h2 histogram oracle parity suite —
159	  tests/oracle.rs with seeded splitmix64 streams (no rand
160	  dep). vs iopsystems `histogram`: **identical counts
161	  arrays** on uniform streams over four configs plus
162	  power-of-two boundary walk — index math is bit-identical.

... [267 lines truncated] ...

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels (current)
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules
[6]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/ceee76323d71 "ceee76323d71b63dce642bdcb172563425d2f54f"
[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"

as the h2 output notation. `examples/h2demo.rs` is left for the

- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels — `src/bands.rs`,
  the first report module, ported from iiac-perf's `bands.rs` and
  reshaped for `no_std`/no-alloc:
  - One `Boundary` enum (`Min`/`Z(k)`/`P(d)`/`N(k)`/`Max`) instead
    of the planned `Boundary` + `BoundaryKind` pair — the kind
    derives everything (fraction, rank, labels), so a wrapper
    struct had nothing left to hold.
  - Fences are integer rationals: `fraction()` returns
    `(num, den)` and `rank(total)` is `total * num / den` in
    u128 — exact where iiac-perf's `(pct * total).floor()` is
    approximate, and no `floor`/`powi` from `std`.
  - Labels write into any `core::fmt::Write` (`write_zpn`,
    `write_frac`, `write_label`, `write_trim_name`) instead of
    returning `String`; `Both` pads zpn to 3 as before.
  - `Ladder` generates boundaries on demand from `(z_depth,
    n_depth)` — no stored table; depths validated to `2..=19`
    (new `Error::BandDepth`), `pow10` saturates as a backstop.
  - Tests pin the z4/n10 ladder to iiac-perf's documented
    boundary list verbatim, rational monotonicity by
    cross-multiplication, exact ranks (incl. `u64::MAX`-scale
    totals), and the demo's z4/n8 depths (21 boundaries: its
    19 fences plus min/max).

Work complete for `0.1.3-2`. Full validation green: 31 lib tests, 3 oracle, 1 doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std on four bare-metal targets, install.

**What changed**

- `src/bands.rs` (new, ~470 lines) — the first report module, ported from iiac-perf's `bands.rs` and reshaped for `no_std`/no-alloc:
  - `Boundary` enum: `Min` / `Z(k)` / `P(d)` / `N(k)` / `Max`, with `fraction() -> (num, den)` as exact integer rationals, `rank(total)` via u128 (no float `floor`), `pct()` for mid-rank consumers.
  - Label writing into any `core::fmt::Write` — `write_zpn`, `write_frac` (with `_` grouping), `write_label` (the `Both` style pads zpn to 3, min/max collapse), `write_trim_name` — no `String` anywhere.
  - `Ladder::new(z_depth, n_depth)` generates boundaries on demand (`get`/`iter`, `ExactSizeIterator`); no stored table.
  - `BandLabels` (`Zpn`/`Frac`/`Both`) with `as_str` for header metadata; no clap dependency, unlike iiac-perf's.
- `src/config.rs` — new `Error::BandDepth` (depths outside `2..=19` rejected; above 19, `10^depth` leaves u64).
- `src/lib.rs` — exports `BandLabels`, `Boundary`, `Ladder`, `LadderIter`.
- Tests pin the z4/n10 ladder **verbatim** against iiac-perf's documented boundary list (both label styles), rational monotonicity by cross-multiplication, exact ranks including a `u64::MAX`-total case, `Both`-label shapes, depth validation, and the demo's z4/n8 depths.
- Bookkeeping: version `0.1.3-2`, ladder rung marked done, chores As-built rung appended.

**One deviation from the plan, flagged:** the ladder rung said `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`. I folded them into the single `Boundary` enum — the kind derives everything (fraction, rank, labels), so a wrapper struct had nothing left to hold. Recorded in the rung and chores.

**Delegation note:** the `-0`/`-1` backfill ran on a Haiku subagent in parallel and verified clean — TODO.md ladder refs `[[6]]`/`[[7]]`, chores `[[13]]`/`[[14]]`, all four URL definitions in place.

Ready for review.

message.content:
Hmmmmm, actually my thoughts was that the printing of the report would use
std but my thought was that contents of the struct could be created and retuned
by a function that would be no_std but the output would use std println ...

Thoughts, no actions?

content.text:
No actions — here's my thinking. The good news: what you describe **is** the architecture being built. Let me separate the three layers, because the labels are the one place where it looks like printing crept into no_std land, and there's a specific reason for it.

## The three layers

- **Numbers (no_std)** — `Boundary::fraction()`, `rank()`, and soon `Band`/`BandTable` in `-4`/`-5`: structs holding first/last/count/mean/variance per band. Built and returned by no_std functions. This is exactly your "contents of the struct created and returned by a no_std function."
- **Text rendering (no_std-*capable*)** — turning a `BandTable` into characters: column widths, padding, the label strings. Written against `core::fmt::Write` (a sink any target can supply).
- **Printing (std)** — `println!` to stdout. The `std`-gated convenience calls the renderer and prints the result. This is your "output would use std println."

Under std the middle layer is invisible: you render into a `String` (which implements `core::fmt::Write`) and `println!` it. Your mental model holds exactly.

## Why the labels don't just "return a string"

A no_std-no-alloc function **can't return a `String`** — the type doesn't exist without an allocator. So a computed label like `0.999_999_9` (depends on runtime depth) has only three possible shapes in no_std land:

- a fixed-size buffer + length (`[u8; 16]`, awkward for callers),
- a `&'static str` table (impossible for runtime-chosen depths without generating every case),
- **written into a caller-supplied sink** — `write_zpn(&mut out)`.

The sink is the idiomatic no-alloc way of "returning" text; `fmt::Write` is core's built-in trait for exactly this, and `String` implements it. So the std caller writes two lines and never notices the machinery.

## Why make the middle layer no_std at all, rather than std-only

This was your "everything practical stays no_std" directive, and it earns its keep on one concrete future: **tprobe on an embedded target**. A Cortex-M box that wants to emit a band table over a serial port or RTT has no stdout — but it has a sink. If rendering were std-only (`format!`, `println!` inside), the embedded consumer would have to rewrite the renderer, which is the four-copies problem this cycle exists to kill. Keeping the renderer sink-generic costs little (a width-measuring pass instead of buffering rows) and means only the final `println!` is std.

If that future never materializes, the cost was small; if it does, the renderer is already there.

**The plain version:** you pictured "no_std builds the data, std prints it" — that's precisely the plan, and the data-building parts landed in this commit. The only subtlety is the label text: bare-metal Rust has no string type to return, so instead of *returning* text, the code *writes* text into whatever the caller hands it. On a normal PC, that "whatever" is just a String, and `println!` finishes the job — exactly your model, with one extra trick underneath so an embedded board could someday hand it a serial port instead.

Happy to walk through how `-5`/`-6` split `BandTable` (pure numbers) from the renderer if you want more detail before we continue.

message.content: But currently it has a bunch of write_* using write! IMO that's not no_std although I do see it core. My thought is the struct would be moved via a "serial port" to a "service" that would then render it/save it/ ... Thoughts

content.text:
No actions — thoughts, in three parts.

## Is `write!` no_std? Technically yes — but that's not really your question

`write!` against `core::fmt::Write` is genuinely `no_std`: `core::fmt` lives in `core`, works on bare metal, allocates nothing. The check script proves it — `bands.rs` compiles for `thumbv7m-none-eabi` today.

But your objection is architectural, not technical: *should a device be rendering text at all?* In your model — struct goes over a serial port, a **service** renders/saves — the answer is no. The device's job is producing compact data; presentation belongs to the host. That's a cleaner split than "rendering that happens to be no_std-capable," and it matches the buffer-swap servicing model already in ARCHITECTURE.md: the probe hands off *data*, analysis happens elsewhere. Your serial-port picture is that same hand-off stretched across a wire.

## What that model implies about the wire artifact

Worth noticing: in the ship-to-a-service world, the natural thing to send is not the `BandTable` — it's the **histogram itself** (two config bytes + the counts array). That's the smallest *sufficient* statistic: bands, quantiles, means, trims can all be derived host-side, and the service can re-analyze with different ladder depths later. A `BandTable` is derived, lossy (midpoint means), and locks in the ladder choice at the device. So the layering becomes:

- **Device (no_std):** record; hand off `(Config, counts)`. Already exactly what the config/storage split and `into_counts()` were built for.
- **Service (std):** build `BandTable` from the counts, render, save. The band/stats modules still earn their keep here — they're the service's analysis library — and they stay `no_std`-*capable* for free, which also serves the host-only consumers (iiac-perf, the demo) that record and render in the same process.

The wire format itself is a future cycle (HdrHistogram V2 compatibility is already in Ideas; `Boundary` is two bytes, `Config` is two bytes — everything here is trivially serializable when that lands).

## Where the `write_*` methods should live, then

Your instinct points at a real blemish: label-writing is *rendering*, and it currently sits on the data type in `bands.rs`. Two defensible placements:

- **Keep as-is.** They're just methods; a device that never renders never calls them and the linker strips them. Zero cost, but the module mixes data and presentation.
- **Move them to `report.rs` when `-6` lands** — my recommendation. `bands.rs` keeps pure data + math (`fraction`, `rank`, `pct`); all text (labels included) lives in the render module. That implements your device/service split at the module boundary, so the code structure *teaches* the architecture. Nothing between now and `-6` needs labels (`-3` band assignment, `-4` stats, `-5` table are all numbers), so the move costs nothing and no churn happens twice.

Whether `report.rs` then stays `no_std`-capable or goes std-only becomes a low-stakes choice: I'd keep it `core::fmt::Write`-based since it costs nothing and stays testable, but with rendering acknowledged as host-side work, I'd stop *justifying* it by embedded output.

**The plain version:** the label-writing code does run on bare metal, so it's technically fine — but you're right that it's *printing work* living in the *data* module. Your picture — device sends raw numbers over a wire, a host program turns them into tables — is the better architecture, and the crate's existing design (borrowed storage, detachable counts) already points that way. The fix is organizational: when the rendering module lands, the label code moves there, leaving the band module pure numbers. And in that world the thing worth sending over the serial port is the raw histogram itself, since everything else can be recomputed on the host.

If you approve, I'll fold "move `write_*` into the render module" into the `-6` step and record the device/service framing as a chores design subsection at that point.

message.content: does folding the move into -6 does that mean we keep the write_/write! for now or what?

content.text:
Right — that was the ambiguity. "Fold into `-6`" meant: commit `-2` now **with** the `write_*` methods in `bands.rs`, then relocate them to `report.rs` four steps later. Having said it out loud: that's the weaker option, because `-2` is still uncommitted — landing code in a spot we've already decided is wrong, just to move it in the same cycle, is pointless history churn.

The better shape, given nothing is committed yet:

- **Strip the label code out of `-2` now.** `bands.rs` commits as pure data + math: `Boundary`, `Ladder`, `fraction()`, `rank()`, `pct()`. Rung retitles to `feat: no_std band ladder`.
- **Park the label code for `-6`.** `write_zpn`/`write_frac`/`write_label`/`write_trim_name`, `write_grouped`, the `BandLabels` enum, and their tests are already written and validated — I'd save them to a scratchpad file so `-6` starts from tested code instead of memory, and they land once, in `report.rs`, where rendering belongs.
- **Tests adjust:** `-2` keeps the ladder-structure pins (variant sequence against iiac-perf's ladder, rational monotonicity, exact ranks, depth validation) by comparing `Boundary` values instead of rendered strings; the string-level pins move to `-6` with the labels.

Result: every commit lands code in its final home, `bands.rs` never contains presentation, and your device/service split is the module boundary from the first commit.

Want me to do that? (Strip + park + retitle, then re-validate and it's ready for review.)

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: one `Boundary` enum (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder`
    generated from two tail depths, `BandLabels` with alloc-free
    label writing
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

# References

[1]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#deferred-buffer-swap-servicing-model
[2]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-h2-histogram-core
[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total
[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules
[6]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/ceee76323d71 "ceee76323d71b63dce642bdcb172563425d2f54f"
[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"

input.old_string:
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels — `src/bands.rs`,
  the first report module, ported from iiac-perf's `bands.rs` and
  reshaped for `no_std`/no-alloc:
  - One `Boundary` enum (`Min`/`Z(k)`/`P(d)`/`N(k)`/`Max`) instead
    of the planned `Boundary` + `BoundaryKind` pair — the kind
    derives everything (fraction, rank, labels), so a wrapper
    struct had nothing left to hold.
  - Fences are integer rationals: `fraction()` returns
    `(num, den)` and `rank(total)` is `total * num / den` in
    u128 — exact where iiac-perf's `(pct * total).floor()` is
    approximate, and no `floor`/`powi` from `std`.
  - Labels write into any `core::fmt::Write` (`write_zpn`,
    `write_frac`, `write_label`, `write_trim_name`) instead of
    returning `String`; `Both` pads zpn to 3 as before.
  - `Ladder` generates boundaries on demand from `(z_depth,
    n_depth)` — no stored table; depths validated to `2..=19`
    (new `Error::BandDepth`), `pow10` saturates as a backstop.
  - Tests pin the z4/n10 ladder to iiac-perf's documented
    boundary list verbatim, rational monotonicity by
    cross-multiplication, exact ranks (incl. `u64::MAX`-scale
    totals), and the demo's z4/n8 depths (21 boundaries: its
    19 fences plus min/max).

Done — `-2` is now pure data, all green (29 lib + 3 oracle + 1 doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4 targets, install).

**What changed in the strip**

- `src/bands.rs` — down to `Boundary` (fraction/rank/pct), `Ladder`, `LadderIter`. All text is gone: `BandLabels`, `write_zpn`/`write_frac`/`write_label`/`write_trim_name`, `write_grouped`, and the `core::fmt` import. Module doc now states the boundary is the wire-friendly artifact and rendering is the render module's job.
- Tests pin the z4/n10 ladder as a **variant sequence** (`[Min, Z(4), … N(10), Max]`) instead of rendered strings — same iiac-perf pin, no text involved. String-level label tests went with the parked code.
- `src/lib.rs` — `BandLabels` export removed.
- **`tmp/` + `.gitignore`** — new git-ignored `/tmp` in the repo, holding `bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs` (the full validated labels version to resurrect at `-6`).
- Ladder/chores rungs retitled to `feat: no_std band ladder`; the `-6` rung now names the parked file, and the chores rung records the device/service reasoning for the pull.

Ready for review.

message.content: When should have example h2demo use band.rs?

content.text:
Per the ladder: at **`0.1.3-7`** (`refactor: h2demo on library report path`) — and I'd hold to that rather than adopt piecemeal. Reasoning:

- **What the demo needs isn't just the ladder.** Its ~200 lines decompose into four things the cycle lands one at a time: fences (`-2`, done), band accumulation (`-3` + `-5`), mean/variance (`-4`), and the printed table with labels (`-6`). The demo's `FENCES` array isn't only fractions — each entry is `(label, fraction_text, fraction)`, and the label/text halves are exactly the rendering that's parked for `-6`. Adopting `bands.rs` today would split one table into two sources of truth: fractions from the library, label strings still demo-local. That's a worse halfway state than the current self-contained copy.

- **The byte-identical gate wants one swap.** `-7`'s contract is "demo output identical to today's at z4/n8 depths." One rewrite → one diff → one gate. Partial adoptions would either need the gate run repeatedly or risk a silent drift mid-cycle (e.g. the demo's `(fraction * total).floor()` in f64 versus `Ladder::rank`'s exact integer math — almost certainly equal at 1M samples, but "almost certainly" is what the single gated swap avoids having to argue about).

- **The demo is the integration test.** Landing `-3`..`-6` first, each with its own unit tests, then swapping the demo in one commit makes `-7` a genuine end-to-end check of the whole report path — the same path iiac-perf and tprobe would consume. If `-7` can't reproduce today's table from the library pieces, that's the signal an API gap slipped through, caught before any external adopter hits it.

So: `bands.rs` sits unused by the demo for four more rungs, on purpose. The `-2` tests already pin it against both iiac-perf's ladder and the demo's z4/n8 shape, so it can't drift meanwhile.

Ready to move on to `-3` (band assignment trait) whenever you are — or the `-2` commit description first, since the strip is done and validated.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist

[5]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#feat-no_std-band-report-modules

[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-2"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]
name = "record"
harness = false

1	# Chores-01
2	
3	Chores-XX files use [Prose form](../../AGENTS.md#prose-form). They
4	contain discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible
5	markdown. There is also a [TODO.md](../../TODO.md) file that tracks
6	tasks and in general there should be a chore section for each task
7	with the why and how this task will be completed.
8	
9	Each task's `##` header is its commit title (see
10	[Conventional-commit shape](../../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit)),
11	with a `Commits:` line first under it — empty until backfilled —
12	then the narrative: conceptual bullets, never a per-file edit
13	list (see
14	[Chores conventions](../../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions)).
15	
16	## feat: no_std h2 histogram core
17	
18	Commits: [[10]]
19	
20	A `no_std`, no-alloc, HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram
21	crate, to become tprobe's core recording structure. tprobe's
22	chores-01 (its 0.1.0-8 decision) surveyed crates.io and found
23	no fit — `hdrhistogram` 7.5.4 is std-only, iopsystems
24	`histogram` is heap-backed, and the `no_std` options have the
25	wrong bucketing — and decided to hand-roll; this repo is that
26	hand-roll promoted to its own crate. Cycle runs on branch
27	`0.1.0-no-std-hdrhistogram` with a non-ff merge close-out
28	(manual jj steps until vc-x1 grows the capability, after its
29	current refactoring).
30	
31	### Founding discussion (2026-07-21)
32	
33	The founding conversation (feasibility → sizing → plan) landed
34	these decisions; sizing detail and the API sketch live in
35	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md), not restated here.
36	
37	- Feasibility: the record path is clz + shift + increment —
38	  integer-only, O(1), trivially `no_std`. The std-bound parts
39	  of existing implementations (dynamic sizing, auto-resize,
40	  compressed wire format, f64 conveniences) are separable and
41	  deferred or feature-gated.
42	- h2 parameterization `(grouping_power, max_value_power)`
43	  over the original `(lowest, highest, sigfigs)` — same
44	  bucketing, cleaner integer math, `const fn`-friendly
45	  bucket-count formula. Config chosen per probe instance.
46	- Config / counts-storage split, core borrowing
47	  `&mut [Count]` — the load-bearing choice; keeps the
48	  deferred buffer-swap model open without reshaping the type.
49	- u32 saturating counts as default. We think monitoring
50	  bounds per-histogram totals (run length now, snapshot
51	  interval later), so u64 is reserved for run-forever
52	  accumulation.
53	- Lifecycle for 0.1.0: one histogram per probe,
54	  run-to-completion, analysis at the end. No snapshot /
55	  reset / swap API yet.
56	- Validation via two oracles: iopsystems `histogram` for
57	  exact index parity (same scheme), `hdrhistogram` 7 for
58	  quantile parity within equivalent-value tolerance; plus
59	  exhaustive small-config walks and a `no_std` target build.
60	- Demo ships as `examples/h2demo.rs`, installable via
61	  `cargo install --path . --example h2demo` (cargo installs
62	  example targets with `--example`).
63	- Readout bar: iiac-perf's band table (z/p/n bands,
64	  first/last/range/count/mean, trimmed mean/stdev) must be
65	  derivable from the bucket iterator — drives cumulative
66	  counts in the iteration API; `h2demo` prints that table.
67	- Parked open questions: hot-path extras — exact min/max and
68	  running sum for exact mean — decided on bench data at
69	  0.1.0-8; f64-vs-integer quantile input (decide at
70	  0.1.0-5); crate rename before any publish.
71	
72	### Sizing analysis
73	
74	A sample costs zero bytes — footprint is fixed at creation:
75	`bytes = (n−g+1)·2ᵍ·sizeof(C)`, ~250 B to ~250 KB across
76	realistic configs (table in
77	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md#size-tradeoffs)).
78	We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot — error is on
79	the value axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× doesn't
80	need 2-sigfig fidelity — putting a 100-probe process near
81	1 MB, noise on a host and shrinkable for embedded.
82	
83	### Deferred: buffer-swap servicing model
84	
85	The intended growth path once run-to-completion is limiting:
86	each probe raises a 1-bit "needs service soon" signal (a
87	watermark on total count or a near-saturated bucket); a
88	background task hands the probe a fresh zeroed counts buffer
89	and takes the full one for analysis. No hard decisions made —
90	the config/storage split is the only accommodation. Watermark
91	semantics, signal transport, and hand-off protocol are all
92	open; tracked as the "Buffer-swap servicing model" Todo.
93	
94	### Record-cost bench and the hot-path-extras decision
95	
96	`benches/record.rs` (hand-rolled, harness=false; g=7 n=30,
97	8M-value heavy-tailed stream, best of 3, 3900X, unpinned —
98	indicative, not a lab record):
99	
100	- raw streaming store 0.87 ns/record
101	- histogram u32 2.57 ns/record
102	- histogram u32 + min/max/sum extras 3.29 ns/record
103	- hdrhistogram (2 sigfig) 4.95 ns/record
104	
105	**Decision: extras stay off the hot path.** Exact min/max and
106	running sum would cost ~+28% per record; band tables already
107	get bucket-resolution first/last and midpoint means from the
108	iterator (h2demo proves the readout), so the default record
109	path keeps only the bucket increment + total. Revisit only if
110	a consumer needs exact mean/min/max — that would be an opt-in
111	wrapper, not a change to `record`. We think the histogram-vs-
112	raw-store gap (2.6 vs 0.9 ns) overstates the field cost: the
113	raw store here is cache-warm and the histogram price buys a
114	fixed footprint, per tprobe's round-3 verdict.
115	
116	### As-built ladder
117	
118	- [[1]] 0.1.0-0 chore: h2 histogram plan capture — the
119	  founding-conversation capture: this section, README goals,
120	  ARCHITECTURE + size table, TODO ladder;
121	  version.toml → 0.1.0-0.
122	- [[2]] 0.1.0-1 chore: scaffold h2 histogram crate —
123	  CargoRust.toml renamed to Cargo.toml (name
124	  `histogram-no-std`, `std` feature, version-of-record moves
125	  here at 0.1.0-1); version.toml retired; src/lib.rs
126	  skeleton with module doc + `no_std` gate.
127	- [[3]] 0.1.0-2 feat: h2 histogram config and index math —
128	  `Config` (validated powers, `const fn` bucket count /
129	  value→index / index→range), `Error`; exhaustive
130	  small-config walk proves indices monotone and ranges an
131	  exact partition; over-range clamps to top bucket, n=64
132	  edge covered.
133	- [[4]] 0.1.0-3 feat: h2 histogram record path — `Counter`
134	  trait (u8..u64, saturating, u32 default) and the
135	  borrowed-storage `Histogram`:
136	  - `new` checks storage length and *recomputes* `total`
137	    from the counts, so a slice handed back by a future
138	    swap servicer rebinds consistently;
139	  - `record`/`record_n` never panic (`get_mut` guarded by
140	    the tested index invariant); `into_counts` releases the
141	    borrow — the swap hand-off shape.
142	- [[5]] 0.1.0-4 feat: h2 histogram owned-array wrapper —
143	  `HistogramArray<N, C>` owns `[C; N]`; N is explicit
144	  (stable Rust can't derive it from Config powers) and
145	  checked in `new`; record path shared with the borrowed
146	  type via `record_into`; `as_histogram()` gives one
147	  analysis surface for both storage shapes (carries the
148	  crate's first non-test `unwrap`, `// OK:` justified —
149	  constructor-checked invariant).
150	- [[6]] 0.1.0-5 feat: h2 histogram quantile merge and iter —
151	  `analysis` module shared by both storage shapes: `Bucket` /
152	  `Buckets` iterator carrying **cumulative** counts (the
153	  band-table requirement), `quantile` (upper-bound
154	  convention — hdrhistogram's highest-equivalent — chosen
155	  for oracle parity; integer ceil, no std float intrinsics),
156	  `merge_from` (saturating, `ConfigMismatch` on unequal
157	  configs). Decision recorded: f64 quantile input kept.
158	- [[7]] 0.1.0-6 test: h2 histogram oracle parity suite —
159	  tests/oracle.rs with seeded splitmix64 streams (no rand
160	  dep). vs iopsystems `histogram`: **identical counts
161	  arrays** on uniform streams over four configs plus
162	  power-of-two boundary walk — index math is bit-identical.

85	## Todo
86	
87	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
88	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then

90	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer

99	1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
100	   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
101	   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
102	   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
103	   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
104	   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
105	2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
106	   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
107	   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
108	   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
109	3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
110	   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
111	   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
112	   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
113	   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
114	   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
115	   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
116	   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
117	   first. See [[5]].

122	  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
123	- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
124	- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
125	  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
126	- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (current)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"
[8]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/a6f7444a0bf7 "a6f7444a0bf72c547cd9c286c914477fd9680970"

input.new_string:
- Tests pin the z4/n10 ladder to iiac-perf's documented
    boundary sequence, rational monotonicity by
    cross-multiplication, exact ranks (incl. `u64::MAX`-scale
    totals), and the demo's z4/n8 depths (21 boundaries: its
    19 fences plus min/max).
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait — `BandAssign` in
  `bands.rs`, distributing a bucket stream into a ladder's
  bands, with both source conventions as impls:
  - `Band` is the accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum,
    midpoint mass) — band `i` spans `(boundary i, boundary
    i+1]`, labeled by the upper fence, matching both sources.
  - `RankSplit` (the demo's convention): a bucket's rank span
    splits across every fence it crosses — band counts are
    exact rank spans; keeps walk state, fresh value per pass.
  - `MidRank` (iiac-perf's): whole bucket to the band holding
    its Hazen mid-rank, right-closed. The compare is integer
    (u128 cross-multiply against the fence rational) — exact
    where iiac-perf's f64 `pct` compare has ulp slack;
    saturating backstop far beyond practical totals.
  - Tests pin a legitimate disagreement (one bucket spanning
    the whole run: RankSplit spreads per-fence, MidRank drops
    all in p50), right-closed fence cases mirroring
    iiac-perf's `band_index` test, a full histogram pass where
    the two split the run's top differently, and fold
    semantics (bounds, midpoint mass, empty buckets inert).

content.text:
Work complete for `0.1.3-3`. Validation green: 33 lib tests (4 new), 3 oracle, 1 doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4 targets, install.

**What changed**

- `src/bands.rs` — the assignment layer, appended to the band module (still pure data/math, no text):
  - `Band` — the per-band accumulator: `first`/`last` (contributing buckets' value bounds), `count`, `weighted_sum` (midpoint mass). Band `i` spans `(boundary i, boundary i+1]`, labeled by the upper fence — the convention both sources share.
  - `BandAssign` trait — one call per bucket over a full `Buckets` pass; implementations may keep walk state, so a fresh value per pass.
  - `RankSplit` (demo's convention) — splits a bucket's rank span across every fence it crosses; band counts are exact rank spans. Ported from `build_bands`' lockstep walk, driven by `Boundary::rank` instead of a fence array.
  - `MidRank` (iiac-perf's convention) — whole bucket to the band holding its Hazen mid-rank, right-closed. The compare is **integer**: u128 cross-multiply against the fence rational, exact where iiac-perf's f64 `pct` compare has ulp slack (saturating backstop in regimes far beyond practical totals).
- `src/lib.rs` — exports `Band`, `BandAssign`, `MidRank`, `RankSplit`.
- Tests: the requested disagreement pin (one bucket spanning the whole run — `RankSplit` spreads `[1,9,10,…,10,9,1]` across all 12 bands, `MidRank` drops all 100 in p50); right-closed fence cases mirroring iiac-perf's `band_index` test; a full histogram pass where the conventions split the run's top differently; fold semantics.

One test expectation of mine was wrong on first run (I forgot values 8–9 share a bucket once the g=2 exact region ends, making that bucket's mid-rank exactly 0.9 → p90 right-closed); fixed the expectation, and it now documents that edge nicely.

Backfill of `-2` (Haiku agent): `[[8]]` in TODO.md, `[[15]]` in chores, both with URL + 40-hex refs — verified.

Bookkeeping: version `0.1.3-3`, rung marked done, chores rung appended.

Ready for review.

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-3"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]
name = "record"
harness = false

76	    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
77	- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
78	  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's

82	    ladder as a visible change

86	## Todo
87	
88	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
89	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
90	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.

100	1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
101	   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
102	   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
103	   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one

113	   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
114	   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into

117	   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
118	   first. See [[5]].
