2	
3	Chores-XX files use [Prose form](../../AGENTS.md#prose-form). They
4	contain discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible
5	markdown. There is also a [TODO.md](../../TODO.md) file that tracks
6	tasks and in general there should be a chore section for each task
7	with the why and how this task will be completed.
8	
9	Each task's `##` header is its commit title (see
10	[Conventional-commit shape](../../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit)),
11	with a `Commits:` line first under it — empty until backfilled —
12	then the narrative: conceptual bullets, never a per-file edit
13	list (see
14	[Chores conventions](../../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions)).
15	
16	## feat: no_std h2 histogram core
17	
18	Commits: [[10]]
19	
20	A `no_std`, no-alloc, HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram
21	crate, to become tprobe's core recording structure. tprobe's
22	chores-01 (its 0.1.0-8 decision) surveyed crates.io and found
23	no fit — `hdrhistogram` 7.5.4 is std-only, iopsystems
24	`histogram` is heap-backed, and the `no_std` options have the
25	wrong bucketing — and decided to hand-roll; this repo is that
26	hand-roll promoted to its own crate. Cycle runs on branch
27	`0.1.0-no-std-hdrhistogram` with a non-ff merge close-out
28	(manual jj steps until vc-x1 grows the capability, after its
29	current refactoring).
30	
31	### Founding discussion (2026-07-21)
32	
33	The founding conversation (feasibility → sizing → plan) landed
34	these decisions; sizing detail and the API sketch live in
35	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md), not restated here.
36	
37	- Feasibility: the record path is clz + shift + increment —
38	  integer-only, O(1), trivially `no_std`. The std-bound parts
39	  of existing implementations (dynamic sizing, auto-resize,
40	  compressed wire format, f64 conveniences) are separable and
41	  deferred or feature-gated.
42	- h2 parameterization `(grouping_power, max_value_power)`
43	  over the original `(lowest, highest, sigfigs)` — same
44	  bucketing, cleaner integer math, `const fn`-friendly
45	  bucket-count formula. Config chosen per probe instance.
46	- Config / counts-storage split, core borrowing
47	  `&mut [Count]` — the load-bearing choice; keeps the
48	  deferred buffer-swap model open without reshaping the type.
49	- u32 saturating counts as default. We think monitoring
50	  bounds per-histogram totals (run length now, snapshot
51	  interval later), so u64 is reserved for run-forever
52	  accumulation.
53	- Lifecycle for 0.1.0: one histogram per probe,
54	  run-to-completion, analysis at the end. No snapshot /
55	  reset / swap API yet.
56	- Validation via two oracles: iopsystems `histogram` for
57	  exact index parity (same scheme), `hdrhistogram` 7 for
58	  quantile parity within equivalent-value tolerance; plus
59	  exhaustive small-config walks and a `no_std` target build.
60	- Demo ships as `examples/h2demo.rs`, installable via
61	  `cargo install --path . --example h2demo` (cargo installs
62	  example targets with `--example`).
63	- Readout bar: iiac-perf's band table (z/p/n bands,
64	  first/last/range/count/mean, trimmed mean/stdev) must be
65	  derivable from the bucket iterator — drives cumulative
66	  counts in the iteration API; `h2demo` prints that table.
67	- Parked open questions: hot-path extras — exact min/max and
68	  running sum for exact mean — decided on bench data at
69	  0.1.0-8; f64-vs-integer quantile input (decide at
70	  0.1.0-5); crate rename before any publish.
71	
72	### Sizing analysis
73	
74	A sample costs zero bytes — footprint is fixed at creation:
75	`bytes = (n−g+1)·2ᵍ·sizeof(C)`, ~250 B to ~250 KB across
76	realistic configs (table in
77	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md#size-tradeoffs)).
78	We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot — error is on
79	the value axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× doesn't
80	need 2-sigfig fidelity — putting a 100-probe process near
81	1 MB, noise on a host and shrinkable for embedded.
82	
83	### Deferred: buffer-swap servicing model
84	
85	The intended growth path once run-to-completion is limiting:
86	each probe raises a 1-bit "needs service soon" signal (a
87	watermark on total count or a near-saturated bucket); a
88	background task hands the probe a fresh zeroed counts buffer
89	and takes the full one for analysis. No hard decisions made —
90	the config/storage split is the only accommodation. Watermark
91	semantics, signal transport, and hand-off protocol are all
92	open; tracked as the "Buffer-swap servicing model" Todo.
93	
94	### Record-cost bench and the hot-path-extras decision
95	
96	`benches/record.rs` (hand-rolled, harness=false; g=7 n=30,
97	8M-value heavy-tailed stream, best of 3, 3900X, unpinned —
98	indicative, not a lab record):
99	
100	- raw streaming store 0.87 ns/record
101	- histogram u32 2.57 ns/record
102	- histogram u32 + min/max/sum extras 3.29 ns/record
103	- hdrhistogram (2 sigfig) 4.95 ns/record
104	
105	**Decision: extras stay off the hot path.** Exact min/max and
106	running sum would cost ~+28% per record; band tables already
107	get bucket-resolution first/last and midpoint means from the
108	iterator (h2demo proves the readout), so the default record
109	path keeps only the bucket increment + total. Revisit only if
110	a consumer needs exact mean/min/max — that would be an opt-in
111	wrapper, not a change to `record`. We think the histogram-vs-
112	raw-store gap (2.6 vs 0.9 ns) overstates the field cost: the
113	raw store here is cache-warm and the histogram price buys a
114	fixed footprint, per tprobe's round-3 verdict.
115	
116	### As-built ladder
117	
118	- [[1]] 0.1.0-0 chore: h2 histogram plan capture — the
119	  founding-conversation capture: this section, README goals,
120	  ARCHITECTURE + size table, TODO ladder;
121	  version.toml → 0.1.0-0.
122	- [[2]] 0.1.0-1 chore: scaffold h2 histogram crate —
123	  CargoRust.toml renamed to Cargo.toml (name
124	  `histogram-no-std`, `std` feature, version-of-record moves
125	  here at 0.1.0-1); version.toml retired; src/lib.rs
126	  skeleton with module doc + `no_std` gate.
127	- [[3]] 0.1.0-2 feat: h2 histogram config and index math —
128	  `Config` (validated powers, `const fn` bucket count /
129	  value→index / index→range), `Error`; exhaustive
130	  small-config walk proves indices monotone and ranges an
131	  exact partition; over-range clamps to top bucket, n=64
132	  edge covered.
133	- [[4]] 0.1.0-3 feat: h2 histogram record path — `Counter`
134	  trait (u8..u64, saturating, u32 default) and the
135	  borrowed-storage `Histogram`:
136	  - `new` checks storage length and *recomputes* `total`
137	    from the counts, so a slice handed back by a future
138	    swap servicer rebinds consistently;
139	  - `record`/`record_n` never panic (`get_mut` guarded by
140	    the tested index invariant); `into_counts` releases the
141	    borrow — the swap hand-off shape.
142	- [[5]] 0.1.0-4 feat: h2 histogram owned-array wrapper —
143	  `HistogramArray<N, C>` owns `[C; N]`; N is explicit
144	  (stable Rust can't derive it from Config powers) and
145	  checked in `new`; record path shared with the borrowed
146	  type via `record_into`; `as_histogram()` gives one
147	  analysis surface for both storage shapes (carries the
148	  crate's first non-test `unwrap`, `// OK:` justified —
149	  constructor-checked invariant).
150	- [[6]] 0.1.0-5 feat: h2 histogram quantile merge and iter —
151	  `analysis` module shared by both storage shapes: `Bucket` /
152	  `Buckets` iterator carrying **cumulative** counts (the
153	  band-table requirement), `quantile` (upper-bound
154	  convention — hdrhistogram's highest-equivalent — chosen
155	  for oracle parity; integer ceil, no std float intrinsics),
156	  `merge_from` (saturating, `ConfigMismatch` on unequal
157	  configs). Decision recorded: f64 quantile input kept.
158	- [[7]] 0.1.0-6 test: h2 histogram oracle parity suite —
159	  tests/oracle.rs with seeded splitmix64 streams (no rand
160	  dep). vs iopsystems `histogram`: **identical counts
161	  arrays** on uniform streams over four configs plus
162	  power-of-two boundary walk — index math is bit-identical.

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (current)
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).

content.text:
Work complete for `0.1.3-4`. Validation green: 38 lib tests (5 new), 3 oracle, 1 doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4, install.

**What changed**

- `src/stats.rs` (new) — the summary-stat module, `no_std`:
  - `Stats { count, mean, variance }`, midpoint-weighted, over a rank window `(lo, hi]`. One primitive covers all three report rows: overall `(0, total]`, tail-trimmed `(0, rank(n2)]`, and the future p10–p90 core window (iiac-perf's Todo #2). Windows split buckets by exact rank span, pairing with `RankSplit`.
  - **Variance returned, not stdev** — as agreed; `stdev()` exists under `std` (`variance.sqrt()`), `libm` left open.
  - **Two-pass variance** — mean first, then centered second moment. The test pins the case that justifies it: mean ~1e9, spread 1 — the demo's `sumsq/n − mean²` shortcut returns **0** (the answer lies below the ulp of the squares), while the two-pass form gives 1.0 exactly. My first version of that assertion mischaracterized the failure as "off by hundreds"; it's actually total loss to cancellation, and the test now says so.
  - `from_buckets`/`from_window` take `Fn() -> Iterator` so the two passes re-create the stream (`|| hist.buckets()`).
- `src/lib.rs` — exports `Stats`.
- Tests: exact small set (mean 3, var 2), window trims (order stats of 1..=99, and a p10–p90-shaped core window), a window splitting one bucket's ranks, the cancellation pin, empty/outside windows zeroed.
- Backfill of `-3` (Haiku agent, verified): `[[9]]` TODO, `[[16]]` chores.
- Bookkeeping: version `0.1.3-4`, rung done, chores rung appended.

Ready for review.

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
    are all the same primitive
  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-4"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]
name = "record"
harness = false

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
4	contains near term tasks with a short description and
5	uses links or reference links for more details.
6	
7	## In Progress
8	
9	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
10	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
11	by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
12	"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
13	replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
14	the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
15	`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
16	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
17	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
18	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation
20	
21	At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
22	`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
23	(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).
24	
25	**feat: no_std band report modules**
26	
27	The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
28	`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
29	`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
30	mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
31	more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
32	`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
33	the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
34	`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
35	silently diverges from the others.
36	
37	Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
38	*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
39	reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
40	decision — see [[5]].
41	
42	- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
43	  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
44	    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
45	- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
46	  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
47	    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
48	    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
49	    literals
50	  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
51	    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
52	    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
53	- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
54	  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
55	    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
56	  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
57	  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
58	    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
59	- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
60	  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
61	    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
62	  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
63	  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
64	- [[10]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
65	  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
66	    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
67	    are all the same primitive
68	  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
69	    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
70	- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure (current)
71	  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
72	    over `Buckets`
73	- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
74	  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
75	    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
76	  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
77	    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
78	    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
79	- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
80	  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
81	    at matching ladder depths
82	- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
83	  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
84	    ladder as a visible change
85	- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
86	  - close-out and validation
87	
88	## Todo
89	
90	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
91	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
92	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
93	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
94	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
95	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
96	 number. Long-tail entries
97	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
98	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
99	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
100	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
101	
102	1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
103	   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
104	   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
105	   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
106	   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
107	   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
108	2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
109	   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
110	   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
111	   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
112	3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
113	   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
114	   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
115	   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
116	   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
117	   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
118	   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
119	   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
120	   first. See [[5]].
121	
122	## Ideas
123	
124	- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
125	  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
126	- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
127	- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
128	  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
129	- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
130	
131	## Bugs
132	
133	See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).
134	
135	## Done
136	
137	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
138	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.
139	
140	- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
141	- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
142	- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]
143

148	[3]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#chore-rename-crate-to-h2hist
149	[4]: notes/chores/chores-01.md#perf-record-path-inlining-read-time-total

152	[7]: https://github.com/winksaville/h2hist/commit/7e52a22dc7a7 "7e52a22dc7a7e1488c42a90215c2b2cf4b65af8c"

1	# Chores-01
2	
3	Chores-XX files use [Prose form](../../AGENTS.md#prose-form). They
4	contain discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible
5	markdown. There is also a [TODO.md](../../TODO.md) file that tracks
6	tasks and in general there should be a chore section for each task
7	with the why and how this task will be completed.
8	
9	Each task's `##` header is its commit title (see
10	[Conventional-commit shape](../../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit)),
11	with a `Commits:` line first under it — empty until backfilled —
12	then the narrative: conceptual bullets, never a per-file edit
13	list (see
14	[Chores conventions](../../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions)).
15	
16	## feat: no_std h2 histogram core
17	
18	Commits: [[10]]
19	
20	A `no_std`, no-alloc, HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram
21	crate, to become tprobe's core recording structure. tprobe's
22	chores-01 (its 0.1.0-8 decision) surveyed crates.io and found
23	no fit — `hdrhistogram` 7.5.4 is std-only, iopsystems
24	`histogram` is heap-backed, and the `no_std` options have the
25	wrong bucketing — and decided to hand-roll; this repo is that
26	hand-roll promoted to its own crate. Cycle runs on branch
27	`0.1.0-no-std-hdrhistogram` with a non-ff merge close-out
28	(manual jj steps until vc-x1 grows the capability, after its
29	current refactoring).
30	
31	### Founding discussion (2026-07-21)
32	
33	The founding conversation (feasibility → sizing → plan) landed
34	these decisions; sizing detail and the API sketch live in
35	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md), not restated here.
36	
37	- Feasibility: the record path is clz + shift + increment —
38	  integer-only, O(1), trivially `no_std`. The std-bound parts
39	  of existing implementations (dynamic sizing, auto-resize,
40	  compressed wire format, f64 conveniences) are separable and
41	  deferred or feature-gated.
42	- h2 parameterization `(grouping_power, max_value_power)`
43	  over the original `(lowest, highest, sigfigs)` — same
44	  bucketing, cleaner integer math, `const fn`-friendly
45	  bucket-count formula. Config chosen per probe instance.
46	- Config / counts-storage split, core borrowing
47	  `&mut [Count]` — the load-bearing choice; keeps the
48	  deferred buffer-swap model open without reshaping the type.
49	- u32 saturating counts as default. We think monitoring
50	  bounds per-histogram totals (run length now, snapshot
51	  interval later), so u64 is reserved for run-forever
52	  accumulation.
53	- Lifecycle for 0.1.0: one histogram per probe,
54	  run-to-completion, analysis at the end. No snapshot /
55	  reset / swap API yet.
56	- Validation via two oracles: iopsystems `histogram` for
57	  exact index parity (same scheme), `hdrhistogram` 7 for
58	  quantile parity within equivalent-value tolerance; plus
59	  exhaustive small-config walks and a `no_std` target build.
60	- Demo ships as `examples/h2demo.rs`, installable via
61	  `cargo install --path . --example h2demo` (cargo installs
62	  example targets with `--example`).
63	- Readout bar: iiac-perf's band table (z/p/n bands,
64	  first/last/range/count/mean, trimmed mean/stdev) must be
65	  derivable from the bucket iterator — drives cumulative
66	  counts in the iteration API; `h2demo` prints that table.
67	- Parked open questions: hot-path extras — exact min/max and
68	  running sum for exact mean — decided on bench data at
69	  0.1.0-8; f64-vs-integer quantile input (decide at
70	  0.1.0-5); crate rename before any publish.
71	
72	### Sizing analysis
73	
74	A sample costs zero bytes — footprint is fixed at creation:
75	`bytes = (n−g+1)·2ᵍ·sizeof(C)`, ~250 B to ~250 KB across
76	realistic configs (table in
77	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md#size-tradeoffs)).
78	We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot — error is on
79	the value axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× doesn't
80	need 2-sigfig fidelity — putting a 100-probe process near
81	1 MB, noise on a host and shrinkable for embedded.
82	
83	### Deferred: buffer-swap servicing model
84	
85	The intended growth path once run-to-completion is limiting:
86	each probe raises a 1-bit "needs service soon" signal (a
87	watermark on total count or a near-saturated bucket); a
88	background task hands the probe a fresh zeroed counts buffer
89	and takes the full one for analysis. No hard decisions made —
90	the config/storage split is the only accommodation. Watermark
91	semantics, signal transport, and hand-off protocol are all
92	open; tracked as the "Buffer-swap servicing model" Todo.
93	
94	### Record-cost bench and the hot-path-extras decision
95	
96	`benches/record.rs` (hand-rolled, harness=false; g=7 n=30,
97	8M-value heavy-tailed stream, best of 3, 3900X, unpinned —
98	indicative, not a lab record):
99	
100	- raw streaming store 0.87 ns/record
101	- histogram u32 2.57 ns/record
102	- histogram u32 + min/max/sum extras 3.29 ns/record
103	- hdrhistogram (2 sigfig) 4.95 ns/record
104	
105	**Decision: extras stay off the hot path.** Exact min/max and
106	running sum would cost ~+28% per record; band tables already
107	get bucket-resolution first/last and midpoint means from the
108	iterator (h2demo proves the readout), so the default record
109	path keeps only the bucket increment + total. Revisit only if
110	a consumer needs exact mean/min/max — that would be an opt-in
111	wrapper, not a change to `record`. We think the histogram-vs-
112	raw-store gap (2.6 vs 0.9 ns) overstates the field cost: the
113	raw store here is cache-warm and the histogram price buys a
114	fixed footprint, per tprobe's round-3 verdict.
115	
116	### As-built ladder
117	
118	- [[1]] 0.1.0-0 chore: h2 histogram plan capture — the
119	  founding-conversation capture: this section, README goals,
120	  ARCHITECTURE + size table, TODO ladder;
121	  version.toml → 0.1.0-0.
122	- [[2]] 0.1.0-1 chore: scaffold h2 histogram crate —
123	  CargoRust.toml renamed to Cargo.toml (name
124	  `histogram-no-std`, `std` feature, version-of-record moves
125	  here at 0.1.0-1); version.toml retired; src/lib.rs
126	  skeleton with module doc + `no_std` gate.
127	- [[3]] 0.1.0-2 feat: h2 histogram config and index math —
128	  `Config` (validated powers, `const fn` bucket count /
129	  value→index / index→range), `Error`; exhaustive
130	  small-config walk proves indices monotone and ranges an
131	  exact partition; over-range clamps to top bucket, n=64
132	  edge covered.
133	- [[4]] 0.1.0-3 feat: h2 histogram record path — `Counter`
134	  trait (u8..u64, saturating, u32 default) and the
135	  borrowed-storage `Histogram`:
136	  - `new` checks storage length and *recomputes* `total`
137	    from the counts, so a slice handed back by a future
138	    swap servicer rebinds consistently;
139	  - `record`/`record_n` never panic (`get_mut` guarded by
140	    the tested index invariant); `into_counts` releases the
141	    borrow — the swap hand-off shape.
142	- [[5]] 0.1.0-4 feat: h2 histogram owned-array wrapper —
143	  `HistogramArray<N, C>` owns `[C; N]`; N is explicit
144	  (stable Rust can't derive it from Config powers) and
145	  checked in `new`; record path shared with the borrowed
146	  type via `record_into`; `as_histogram()` gives one
147	  analysis surface for both storage shapes (carries the
148	  crate's first non-test `unwrap`, `// OK:` justified —
149	  constructor-checked invariant).
150	- [[6]] 0.1.0-5 feat: h2 histogram quantile merge and iter —
151	  `analysis` module shared by both storage shapes: `Bucket` /
152	  `Buckets` iterator carrying **cumulative** counts (the
153	  band-table requirement), `quantile` (upper-bound
154	  convention — hdrhistogram's highest-equivalent — chosen
155	  for oracle parity; integer ceil, no std float intrinsics),
156	  `merge_from` (saturating, `ConfigMismatch` on unequal
157	  configs). Decision recorded: f64 quantile input kept.
158	- [[7]] 0.1.0-6 test: h2 histogram oracle parity suite —
159	  tests/oracle.rs with seeded splitmix64 streams (no rand
160	  dep). vs iopsystems `histogram`: **identical counts
161	  arrays** on uniform streams over four configs plus
162	  power-of-two boundary walk — index math is bit-identical.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[10]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
    are all the same primitive
  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure (current)
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

content.text:
Work complete for `0.1.3-5`, all green (43 lib tests — 5 new, oracle, doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4, install).

**What changed**

- `src/table.rs` (new) — `BandTable<CAP>`, the assembled ship-to-a-service structure: bands + `overall()` + `trimmed()` stats + `trim_range()`, all numbers, no text.
  - `CAP` sized at compile time via new `const fn Ladder::band_count()` — same pattern as `Config::total_buckets()` sizing counts arrays.
  - `build(ladder, assigner, || hist.buckets())` is generic over `BandAssign`, so either convention assembles the same structure; it makes several cheap passes (total, assignment, two-pass stats).
  - Trim anchor = the n2 fence: trimmed stats over `(0, rank(n2)]`; `trim_range()` names the populated extent below the cut (the `z4..n2` label data).
- `src/config.rs` — `Error::TableCapacity` (I'd omitted it on the first compile; added).
- `src/lib.rs` — exports `BandTable`.
- Tests: table ≡ manual assignment + `Stats` windows, trim extent naming, MidRank total conservation, capacity rejection, zeroed empty table.
- Backfill of `-4` (Haiku, verified): `[[10]]` TODO, `[[17]]` chores.
- Bookkeeping: version `0.1.3-5`, rung done, chores rung appended.

message.content: One question, we should be able to render a "proper" histogram graph instead of a table, correct?

lastPrompt: One question, we should be able to render a "proper" histogram graph instead of a table, correct?

aiTitle: Plan modular refactoring of support functions

content.text:
Yes — everything needed is already in the structures, and it's arguably a *more* natural rendering than the table:

- **The data is there.** `Buckets` yields every bucket's `(low, high, count)` — that's the density function at full bucket resolution. `BandTable`'s bands are the same thing coarsened to the quantile fences. A bar chart is just: scale each count to a bar length, print.
- **A terminal render is cheap** and fits the same sink-based design as the table renderer — e.g. horizontal bars, one row per (non-empty) bucket or per band:

  ```
  [64..71]     1,204 ██████████▌
  [72..79]    48,310 ████████████████████████████████
  [80..87]    22,101 ██████████████▋
  ```

  Two wrinkles worth deciding when it lands:
  - **Value axis is log-linear** (h2 buckets widen as values grow), so equal-height rows cover unequal value spans — fine for a bucket-per-row bar chart, but a *binned* axis needs care.
  - **Count axis usually wants log scaling or a max-normalized bar**, because latency histograms have a mode thousands of times taller than the tail; linear bars make the tail invisible. hdrhistogram's classic percentile plot solves this differently (CDF vs. log(1−q)), which is also derivable from `cumulative` — both flavors are options.
- **Where it fits:** same place as the table renderer — a `render_*` function over `core::fmt::Write` in the `-6` module (or a follow-on), consuming the existing structures. Nothing in `-5` or earlier needs to change to enable it, which is a good sign the data/render split is right.

I'd scope it out of this cycle (the byte-identical demo gate is about the table) and record it as an Idea — "terminal histogram/bar-chart renderer over Buckets/BandTable; count-axis scaling and CDF-plot variant TBD." Want me to add that line to `TODO.md > ## Ideas` before this push so it rides along, or leave it for later?

message.content: add it to Ideas and push

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[10]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
    are all the same primitive
  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure (done)
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` — bands + overall
    + trimmed stats + trim range, assembled from a re-creatable
    bucket stream; `CAP` from a `const Ladder::band_count()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# no_std-first HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram; the
# core recording structure for ../tprobe. Design and size
# tradeoffs in ARCHITECTURE.md; version-of-record lives here
# (see notes/versioning.md).
[package]
name = "h2hist"
version = "0.1.3-5"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.97"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
description = "no_std no-alloc HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram"
publish = false

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []

# Panicking sites are opt-in: each needs a site `#[allow]` +
# `// OK: …` justification (see AGENTS.md > Code Conventions).
[lints.clippy]
unwrap_used = "warn"
expect_used = "warn"

[dependencies]

# Correctness oracles (tests only): iopsystems `histogram`
# shares the h2 scheme (exact counts parity); `hdrhistogram`
# is the reference implementation (quantile parity within
# equivalent-value tolerance).
[dev-dependencies]
hdrhistogram = { version = "7", default-features = false }
histogram = "1"

# Hand-rolled bench (no criterion): a custom main keeps the
# dev-dep graph small and the loop under our control.
[[bench]]
name = "record"
harness = false

4	contains near term tasks with a short description and
5	uses links or reference links for more details.

10	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
11	by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
12	"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
13	replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
14	the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a

17	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
18	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

21	At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
22	`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
23	(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).
24	
25	**feat: no_std band report modules**
26	
27	The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
28	`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
29	`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
30	mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
31	more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
32	`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
33	the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
34	`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
35	silently diverges from the others.
36	
37	Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
38	*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
39	reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
40	decision — see [[5]].
41	
42	- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
43	  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
44	    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
45	- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
46	  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
47	    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
48	    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
49	    literals
50	  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each

54	  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
55	    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
56	  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
57	  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
58	    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)

60	  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)

66	    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
67	    are all the same primitive

69	    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`

83	- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules

85	    ladder as a visible change

87	  - close-out and validation
88	
89	## Todo
90	
91	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
92	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
93	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
94	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
95	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
96	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
97	 number. Long-tail entries
98	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
99	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
100	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
101	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
102	
103	1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
104	   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
105	   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
106	   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
107	   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
108	   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
109	2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
110	   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
111	   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
112	   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
113	3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
114	   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
115	   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
116	   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
117	   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
118	   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
119	   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
120	   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
121	   first. See [[5]].
122	
123	## Ideas
124	
125	- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
126	  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
127	- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
128	- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
129	  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
130	- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
131	- Terminal histogram / bar-chart renderer over
132	  `Buckets`/`BandTable` (a graph, not a table); count-axis
133	  scaling (log or max-normalized) and a CDF percentile-plot
134	  variant TBD.
135	
136	## Bugs

1	# Chores-01
2	
3	Chores-XX files use [Prose form](../../AGENTS.md#prose-form). They
4	contain discussions and notes on various chores in github compatible
5	markdown. There is also a [TODO.md](../../TODO.md) file that tracks
6	tasks and in general there should be a chore section for each task
7	with the why and how this task will be completed.
8	
9	Each task's `##` header is its commit title (see
10	[Conventional-commit shape](../../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit)),
11	with a `Commits:` line first under it — empty until backfilled —
12	then the narrative: conceptual bullets, never a per-file edit
13	list (see
14	[Chores conventions](../../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions)).
15	
16	## feat: no_std h2 histogram core
17	
18	Commits: [[10]]
19	
20	A `no_std`, no-alloc, HdrHistogram-style log-linear histogram
21	crate, to become tprobe's core recording structure. tprobe's
22	chores-01 (its 0.1.0-8 decision) surveyed crates.io and found
23	no fit — `hdrhistogram` 7.5.4 is std-only, iopsystems
24	`histogram` is heap-backed, and the `no_std` options have the
25	wrong bucketing — and decided to hand-roll; this repo is that
26	hand-roll promoted to its own crate. Cycle runs on branch
27	`0.1.0-no-std-hdrhistogram` with a non-ff merge close-out
28	(manual jj steps until vc-x1 grows the capability, after its
29	current refactoring).
30	
31	### Founding discussion (2026-07-21)
32	
33	The founding conversation (feasibility → sizing → plan) landed
34	these decisions; sizing detail and the API sketch live in
35	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md), not restated here.
36	
37	- Feasibility: the record path is clz + shift + increment —
38	  integer-only, O(1), trivially `no_std`. The std-bound parts
39	  of existing implementations (dynamic sizing, auto-resize,
40	  compressed wire format, f64 conveniences) are separable and
41	  deferred or feature-gated.
42	- h2 parameterization `(grouping_power, max_value_power)`
43	  over the original `(lowest, highest, sigfigs)` — same
44	  bucketing, cleaner integer math, `const fn`-friendly
45	  bucket-count formula. Config chosen per probe instance.
46	- Config / counts-storage split, core borrowing
47	  `&mut [Count]` — the load-bearing choice; keeps the
48	  deferred buffer-swap model open without reshaping the type.
49	- u32 saturating counts as default. We think monitoring
50	  bounds per-histogram totals (run length now, snapshot
51	  interval later), so u64 is reserved for run-forever
52	  accumulation.
53	- Lifecycle for 0.1.0: one histogram per probe,
54	  run-to-completion, analysis at the end. No snapshot /
55	  reset / swap API yet.
56	- Validation via two oracles: iopsystems `histogram` for
57	  exact index parity (same scheme), `hdrhistogram` 7 for
58	  quantile parity within equivalent-value tolerance; plus
59	  exhaustive small-config walks and a `no_std` target build.
60	- Demo ships as `examples/h2demo.rs`, installable via
61	  `cargo install --path . --example h2demo` (cargo installs
62	  example targets with `--example`).
63	- Readout bar: iiac-perf's band table (z/p/n bands,
64	  first/last/range/count/mean, trimmed mean/stdev) must be
65	  derivable from the bucket iterator — drives cumulative
66	  counts in the iteration API; `h2demo` prints that table.
67	- Parked open questions: hot-path extras — exact min/max and
68	  running sum for exact mean — decided on bench data at
69	  0.1.0-8; f64-vs-integer quantile input (decide at
70	  0.1.0-5); crate rename before any publish.
71	
72	### Sizing analysis
73	
74	A sample costs zero bytes — footprint is fixed at creation:
75	`bytes = (n−g+1)·2ᵍ·sizeof(C)`, ~250 B to ~250 KB across
76	realistic configs (table in
77	[ARCHITECTURE.md](../../ARCHITECTURE.md#size-tradeoffs)).
78	We think g=4..6 is the monitoring sweet spot — error is on
79	the value axis, and flagging a p99.9 that moved 10× doesn't
80	need 2-sigfig fidelity — putting a 100-probe process near
81	1 MB, noise on a host and shrinkable for embedded.
82	
83	### Deferred: buffer-swap servicing model
84	
85	The intended growth path once run-to-completion is limiting:
86	each probe raises a 1-bit "needs service soon" signal (a
87	watermark on total count or a near-saturated bucket); a
88	background task hands the probe a fresh zeroed counts buffer
89	and takes the full one for analysis. No hard decisions made —
90	the config/storage split is the only accommodation. Watermark
91	semantics, signal transport, and hand-off protocol are all
92	open; tracked as the "Buffer-swap servicing model" Todo.
93	
94	### Record-cost bench and the hot-path-extras decision
95	
96	`benches/record.rs` (hand-rolled, harness=false; g=7 n=30,
97	8M-value heavy-tailed stream, best of 3, 3900X, unpinned —
98	indicative, not a lab record):
99	
100	- raw streaming store 0.87 ns/record
101	- histogram u32 2.57 ns/record
102	- histogram u32 + min/max/sum extras 3.29 ns/record
103	- hdrhistogram (2 sigfig) 4.95 ns/record
104	
105	**Decision: extras stay off the hot path.** Exact min/max and
106	running sum would cost ~+28% per record; band tables already
107	get bucket-resolution first/last and midpoint means from the
108	iterator (h2demo proves the readout), so the default record
109	path keeps only the bucket increment + total. Revisit only if
110	a consumer needs exact mean/min/max — that would be an opt-in
111	wrapper, not a change to `record`. We think the histogram-vs-
112	raw-store gap (2.6 vs 0.9 ns) overstates the field cost: the
113	raw store here is cache-warm and the histogram price buys a
114	fixed footprint, per tprobe's round-3 verdict.
115	
116	### As-built ladder
117	
118	- [[1]] 0.1.0-0 chore: h2 histogram plan capture — the
119	  founding-conversation capture: this section, README goals,
120	  ARCHITECTURE + size table, TODO ladder;
121	  version.toml → 0.1.0-0.
122	- [[2]] 0.1.0-1 chore: scaffold h2 histogram crate —
123	  CargoRust.toml renamed to Cargo.toml (name
124	  `histogram-no-std`, `std` feature, version-of-record moves
125	  here at 0.1.0-1); version.toml retired; src/lib.rs
126	  skeleton with module doc + `no_std` gate.
127	- [[3]] 0.1.0-2 feat: h2 histogram config and index math —
128	  `Config` (validated powers, `const fn` bucket count /
129	  value→index / index→range), `Error`; exhaustive
130	  small-config walk proves indices monotone and ranges an
131	  exact partition; over-range clamps to top bucket, n=64
132	  edge covered.
133	- [[4]] 0.1.0-3 feat: h2 histogram record path — `Counter`
134	  trait (u8..u64, saturating, u32 default) and the
135	  borrowed-storage `Histogram`:
136	  - `new` checks storage length and *recomputes* `total`
137	    from the counts, so a slice handed back by a future
138	    swap servicer rebinds consistently;
139	  - `record`/`record_n` never panic (`get_mut` guarded by
140	    the tested index invariant); `into_counts` releases the
141	    borrow — the swap hand-off shape.
142	- [[5]] 0.1.0-4 feat: h2 histogram owned-array wrapper —
143	  `HistogramArray<N, C>` owns `[C; N]`; N is explicit
144	  (stable Rust can't derive it from Config powers) and
145	  checked in `new`; record path shared with the borrowed
146	  type via `record_into`; `as_histogram()` gives one
147	  analysis surface for both storage shapes (carries the
148	  crate's first non-test `unwrap`, `// OK:` justified —
149	  constructor-checked invariant).
150	- [[6]] 0.1.0-5 feat: h2 histogram quantile merge and iter —
151	  `analysis` module shared by both storage shapes: `Bucket` /
152	  `Buckets` iterator carrying **cumulative** counts (the
153	  band-table requirement), `quantile` (upper-bound
154	  convention — hdrhistogram's highest-equivalent — chosen
155	  for oracle parity; integer ceil, no std float intrinsics),
156	  `merge_from` (saturating, `ConfigMismatch` on unequal
157	  configs). Decision recorded: f64 quantile input kept.
158	- [[7]] 0.1.0-6 test: h2 histogram oracle parity suite —
159	  tests/oracle.rs with seeded splitmix64 streams (no rand
160	  dep). vs iopsystems `histogram`: **identical counts
161	  arrays** on uniform streams over four configs plus
162	  power-of-two boundary walk — index math is bit-identical.

... [355 lines truncated] ...

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[10]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
    are all the same primitive
  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
- [[11]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure (done)
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` — bands + overall
    + trimmed stats + trim range, assembled from a re-creatable
    bucket stream; `CAP` from a `const Ladder::band_count()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering (current)
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
  - boundary labels land here (`BandLabels`, the `write_*`
    methods and tests parked at `-2` in
    `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs`)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
- Terminal histogram / bar-chart renderer over
  `Buckets`/`BandTable` (a graph, not a table); count-axis
  scaling (log or max-normalized) and a CDF percentile-plot
  variant TBD.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

input.new_string:
- Tests: table equals manual assignment + Stats windows,
    trim extent naming, MidRank total conservation, capacity
    rejection, zeroed empty table.
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering — `src/report.rs`,
  the render side of the device/service split; everything
  writes into `core::fmt::Write`, `no_std`-capable, no alloc:
  - Boundary labels land here as free functions (the `-2`
    parked code, adapted): a label is presentation, so it
    lives with the renderer, not on the data types. String
    pins vs iiac-perf's documented label lists restored.
  - `render_band_table`: header, one row per populated band
    (first/last/range/count/mean), blank line, overall and
    trimmed mean/stdev rows — the demo's shape, proven by a
    snapshot test that renders a fully-predictable table and
    compares byte-for-byte against the demo's own format
    strings (`Layout::DEMO_LEGACY`).
  - `Layout` carries the column widths: `measure()` for snug
    columns (the two-pass width measurement), `DEMO_LEGACY`
    for the demo's historical fixed shape — both feed the
    same renderer, keeping the `-7` byte-identical gate and
    the iiac-perf measured style on one code path.
  - Alloc-free number formatting: `write_commas` (digit
    buffer) and `write_commas_f64` (scaled u128 integer
    rounding — no `round`/`floor` from `std`).
  - stdev cells need a `sqrt`: `std` uses the intrinsic; the
    `no_std` build uses a bit-hack seed + 5 Newton steps. We
    think the fallback is within an ulp or two — invisible at
    report decimals.
  - `Both` in two-cell form prints zpn and fraction as
    separate columns (the demo's `{:<4} {:<13}` shape);
    min/max rows leave the fraction cell empty.
