content.text:
Work complete for `0.1.3-6`. All green: 51 lib tests (8 new), oracle, doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4 targets (exercising the Newton path), install.

**What changed**

- `src/report.rs` (new, ~600 lines) — the render side of the device/service split, everything over `core::fmt::Write`:
  - **Labels landed here** as free functions (`write_zpn`/`write_frac`/`write_label`/`write_trim_name` + `BandLabels`) — the `-2` parked code, adapted from methods to functions since presentation doesn't belong on the data types. The string pins against iiac-perf's documented label lists are restored.
  - **`render_band_table`** — header, one row per populated band (first/last/range/count/mean), blank line, overall + trimmed mean/stdev rows. A snapshot test renders a fully-predictable table (10 × the same value) and compares **byte-for-byte** against expected text built from the demo's own `{:<4} {:<13} {:>14}…` format strings.
  - **`Layout`** — the width knobs. `Layout::measure()` gives snug columns (the two-pass width measurement); `Layout::DEMO_LEGACY` reproduces the demo's fixed shape. One renderer serves both, which is what keeps the `-7` byte-identical gate and iiac-perf's measured style on a single code path.
  - **Alloc-free formatting**: `write_commas` (digit buffer), `write_commas_f64` (scaled u128 rounding — no `round`/`floor`).
  - **stdev cells**: `std` uses `f64::sqrt`; the `no_std` build gets a bit-hack seed + 5 Newton steps. We think the fallback is within an ulp or two — invisible at report decimals. Both paths tested.
- `src/lib.rs` — exports the render surface.
- `tmp/bands-with-labels-parked-for-0.1.3-6.rs` — consumed and deleted.
- Backfill of `-5` (Haiku, verified): `[[11]]` TODO, `[[18]]` chores.
- Bookkeeping: version `0.1.3-6`, rung done, chores rung appended.

One compile fix along the way (`summary_row` missing its return value) and one test-hygiene fix (I initially wrote the snapshot's expected text with a `.replace()` patch — cleaned to a plain expected string).

Ready for review — commit description on your word.

message.content: Would it be more obvious/simpler short if report.rs used std that seems better to me, thoughts no changes.
Would it be more obvious/simpler/shorter if report.rs used std that seems better to me, thoughts no changes.

content.text:
My honest assessment: you're right, and this is the same lesson as the `-2` strip — I argued the no_std renderer "costs little," and now that it's written, the cost is visible and real. Tallying it:

**What no_std-capable rendering actually cost in `report.rs`:**

- `CountWriter` + `measure()` + the `cell()` helper taking *two closures* (one to measure, one to emit) — every aligned cell is written twice through closure gymnastics. Under std: render the cell into a `String` once, take `.len()`, done.
- Hand-rolled `write_commas_f64` (~40 lines of scaled-u128 rounding, clamps, fallbacks) versus iiac-perf's ~12-line version over `format!("{n:.decimals$}")`.
- The Newton `sqrt64` fallback (~20 lines) plus a "we think it's within an ulp" hedge — versus just `f64::sqrt`.
- Alignment by manual `pad()` calls versus `format!("{:>width$}")`, which is both shorter and instantly recognizable.

Rough estimate: a std `report.rs` is **150–200 lines shorter and considerably more obvious** — rows built as `String`s, measured by `.len()`, aligned by `format!` width parameters, exactly the shape iiac-perf's `print_report` already has.

**What no_std rendering buys, revisited:** only the hypothetical on-device text output (serial console, RTT). But your device/service model already settled that — devices ship structs, services render. And I conceded exactly this at the label discussion: "with rendering acknowledged as host-side work, I'd stop justifying it by embedded output." I then wrote a no_std renderer anyway, mostly because the sink-generic style *felt* free. It wasn't.

**What I'd do:** gate the whole module — `#[cfg(feature = "std")] mod report;` — and rewrite its internals on `String`/`format!`. Possibly even simplify the API to `render_band_table(...) -> String`. Nothing else changes: `bands`/`stats`/`table` stay `no_std` (the structures a device ships), and the crate boundary now *states* the architecture — data is `no_std`, text is `std`. Since `-6` is still unpushed, converting now lands it once, in its final shape, with no history churn — the same reasoning you applied at `-2`.

The one thing genuinely lost: a `no_std` target could no longer produce the table text on-device. If that ever becomes real, the git history has this version to resurrect.

**The plain version:** I built the text-rendering to work on bare metal "because it barely costs anything," but the finished code shows it cost a lot of fiddly machinery — double-writing every cell, hand-made number formatting, a homemade square root. Since we already agreed devices send raw data and the host does the printing, all that machinery serves nobody. Making the render module require the standard library would delete the machinery and leave clearer, shorter code — and the module split would then perfectly mirror your architecture.

Your call: convert `-6` to std-only before we commit it (my recommendation), or keep the no_std renderer as written?

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.

3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
- Terminal histogram / bar-chart renderer over
  `Buckets`/`BandTable` (a graph, not a table); count-axis
  scaling (log or max-normalized) and a CDF percentile-plot
  variant TBD.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

The split follows the device/service model: the `no_std` modules
build report *structures* (what a device ships over a wire);
rendering them as text is the `std`-gated `report` module. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- `f64::floor` / `powi` are not in `core` either, so the fence
  ladder is integer rationals: a fence is `num/den` and a
  boundary is `total * num / den` in u128. Exact, and more
  accurate than the `(fraction * total as f64).floor()` the
  demo used.
- Alloc-free rendering measures column widths with a counting
  writer pass rather than buffering formatted rows, and label
  text is written into the sink instead of returned as
  `String`.

input.old_string:
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering — `src/report.rs`,
  the render side of the device/service split; everything
  writes into `core::fmt::Write`, `no_std`-capable, no alloc:
  - Boundary labels land here as free functions (the `-2`
    parked code, adapted): a label is presentation, so it
    lives with the renderer, not on the data types. String
    pins vs iiac-perf's documented label lists restored.
  - `render_band_table`: header, one row per populated band
    (first/last/range/count/mean), blank line, overall and
    trimmed mean/stdev rows — the demo's shape, proven by a
    snapshot test that renders a fully-predictable table and
    compares byte-for-byte against the demo's own format
    strings (`Layout::DEMO_LEGACY`).
  - `Layout` carries the column widths: `measure()` for snug
    columns (the two-pass width measurement), `DEMO_LEGACY`
    for the demo's historical fixed shape — both feed the
    same renderer, keeping the `-7` byte-identical gate and
    the iiac-perf measured style on one code path.
  - Alloc-free number formatting: `write_commas` (digit
    buffer) and `write_commas_f64` (scaled u128 integer
    rounding — no `round`/`floor` from `std`).
  - stdev cells need a `sqrt`: `std` uses the intrinsic; the
    `no_std` build uses a bit-hack seed + 5 Newton steps. We
    think the fallback is within an ulp or two — invisible at
    report decimals.
  - `Both` in two-cell form prints zpn and fraction as
    separate columns (the demo's `{:<4} {:<13}` shape);
    min/max rows leave the fraction cell empty.

# References
input.new_string:
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering — `src/report.rs`,
  the render side of the device/service split, **`std`-only**:
  - A `no_std`-capable renderer was fully drafted first
    (counting-writer width passes, double-write cells,
    hand-rolled scaled-u128 f64 formatting, a Newton `sqrt`
    fallback) and rejected on review: devices ship structs
    and services render, so the ~200 lines of machinery
    served nobody. Gating the module on `std` states the
    architecture at the crate boundary and the code drops to
    plain `String` / `format!` — the shape iiac-perf's
    `print_report` already has.
  - Boundary labels land here as free functions (the `-2`
    parked code, adapted): a label is presentation, so it
    lives with the renderer, not on the data types. String
    pins vs iiac-perf's documented label lists restored.
  - `render_band_table` returns a `String`: header, one row
    per populated band (first/last/range/count/mean), blank
    line, overall and trimmed mean/stdev rows — the demo's
    shape, proven by a snapshot test that renders a
    fully-predictable table and compares byte-for-byte
    against the demo's own format strings
    (`Layout::DEMO_LEGACY`).
  - `Layout` carries the column widths: `measure()` for snug
    columns, `DEMO_LEGACY` for the demo's historical fixed
    shape — both feed the same renderer, keeping the `-7`
    byte-identical gate and the iiac-perf measured style on
    one code path.
  - `fmt_commas` / `fmt_commas_f64` are iiac-perf's shapes
    (format! + regrouping). Noted: format!'s rounding follows
    the true stored double (0.95 stores below the tie and
    prints `0.9` at one decimal) where the demo's
    `(x*10).round()/10` can differ through an intermediate;
    we think no real mean lands on such a tie, and the `-7`
    gate would catch one.
  - `Both` in two-cell form prints zpn and fraction as
    separate columns (the demo's `{:<4} {:<13}` shape);
    min/max rows leave the fraction cell empty.

    buffer) and `write_commas_f64` (scaled u128 integer
    rounding — no `round`/`floor` from `std`).
  - stdev cells need a `sqrt`: `std` uses the intrinsic; the
    `no_std` build uses a bit-hack seed + 5 Newton steps. We
    think the fallback is within an ulp or two — invisible at
    report decimals.
  - `Both` in two-cell form prints zpn and fraction as
    separate columns (the demo's `{:<4} {:<13}` shape);
    min/max rows leave the fraction cell empty.

Converted — `report.rs` is std-only, and it's dramatically simpler. All green (50 lib tests, 3 oracle, 1 doctest, clippy `-D warnings`, no_std × 4 — which now proves the core builds *without* the render module, install).

**What the conversion changed**

- `src/report.rs` — ~600 → ~440 lines, all `String`/`format!`:
  - Deleted outright: `CountWriter`, `measure()`, `pad()`, the double-closure `cell()`, the Newton `sqrt64`, the hand-rolled scaled-u128 `write_commas_f64`.
  - Labels return `String`s: `zpn_label`, `frac_label`, `boundary_label(style)`, `trim_name(style)`.
  - `fmt_commas` / `fmt_commas_f64` are now iiac-perf's exact shapes (`format!` + regrouping) — including the names, which will make its adoption diff trivial.
  - `render_band_table(...) -> String`; alignment by `format!("{:>width$}")`. Snapshot + measured-layout tests unchanged in intent and passing.
- `src/lib.rs` — `#[cfg(feature = "std")]` on the module and its re-exports.
- One behavioral finding, pinned in a test and noted in chores: `format!` rounds the *true stored double* (0.95 stores below the tie → `"0.9"` at one decimal), where the demo's `(x*10).round()/10` can differ through an intermediate. We think no real mean lands on such a tie; the `-7` gate would catch one.
- Docs synced: ARCHITECTURE feature map, TODO problem statement + `-6` rung, chores intro bullet + a rewritten `-6` rung recording the draft-then-reject decision.

  - snapshot test byte-identical to the demo's own format

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

The split follows the device/service model: the `no_std` modules
build report *structures* (what a device ships over a wire);
rendering them as text is the `std`-gated `report` module. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[6]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (done)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[7]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo (done)
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[8]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder (done)
  - `src/bands.rs`: pure data and math — one `Boundary` enum
    (Min/Z/P/N/Max), `Ladder` generated from two tail depths
  - fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
  - label rendering deliberately excluded: text belongs to the
    render module, landing at `-6` (parked in `tmp/`)
- [[9]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait (done)
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank)
  - `Band` accumulator (first/last/count/weighted_sum)
  - tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[10]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance (done)
  - `src/stats.rs`: `Stats` (count/mean/variance) over a rank
    window `(lo, hi]` — overall, tail-trimmed, and core windows
    are all the same primitive
  - two-pass variance, avoiding the `sumsq/n - mean²`
    cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()` under `std`
- [[11]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure (done)
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` — bands + overall
    + trimmed stats + trim range, assembled from a re-creatable
    bucket stream; `CAP` from a `const Ladder::band_count()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering (done)
  - `src/report.rs`, `std`-only: `render_band_table` returns a
    `String`, measured (`Layout::measure`) or fixed
    (`Layout::DEMO_LEGACY`) column widths
  - boundary labels landed here as free functions (`BandLabels`,
    `zpn_label`/`frac_label`/`boundary_label`/`trim_name`);
    parked `tmp/` file consumed and removed
  - `fmt_commas`/`fmt_commas_f64` in iiac-perf's shape; a
    drafted `no_std` renderer was rejected as machinery serving
    nobody (devices ship structs, services render)
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one
   for analysis. Deferred with no hard decisions; the
   config/storage split keeps it open. See [[1]].
2. **tprobe adoption.** Replace `hdrhistogram` in tprobe's
   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.
   The integration cycle runs in tprobe's repo; this entry
   tracks API gaps it surfaces here.
3. **iiac-perf adoption.** Retire the three copies of the
   accumulate-then-render loop (`harness.rs::print_report`,
   `band_table.rs::render`, `probe.rs::report`) onto this
   crate's band/report modules; `Bucket`'s fields are `pub`,
   so iiac-perf maps `hdrhistogram::iter_recorded()` into
   `Bucket`s with no dependency change here. The integration
   cycle runs in iiac-perf's repo; this entry tracks the API
   gaps it surfaces — the `adjusted` column extension point
   first. See [[5]].

## Ideas

- Interval snapshot / reset API (monitoring cadence; makes
  u32-vs-u64 count-width pressure moot).
- Atomic concurrent recording via `portable-atomic`.
- HdrHistogram V2 wire/log format compatibility (zigzag
  LEB128 is `no_std`-fine; compression needs a dep).
- `alloc` feature: Vec-backed storage, auto-resize.
- Terminal histogram / bar-chart renderer over
  `Buckets`/`BandTable` (a graph, not a table); count-axis
  scaling (log or max-normalized) and a CDF percentile-plot
  variant TBD.

## Bugs

See [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](notes/done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: no_std h2 histogram core [[2]]
- chore: rename crate to h2hist [[3]]
- perf: record path inlining, read-time total [[4]]

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
path = '.claude/.vc-config.toml'
text = open(path).read()
old = '''# vc-config: Vibe Coding workspace configuration
#
# workspace-path is this repo's path relative to the workspace root.
# Used to resolve changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /.claude/changeID).
# other-repo is the relative path to the counterpart repo.

[workspace]
path = "/.claude"
other-repo = ".."'''
new = '''# vc-config: Vibe Coding workspace configuration
#
# The symmetric workspace pair: each repo's path relative to the
# workspace root, identical in both repos. Used to resolve
# changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /.claude/changeID).

[workspace]
work = "/"
bot = "/.claude"'''
assert old in text
open(path, 'w').write(text.replace(old, new))
print("bot config updated")
EOF
